title_en: A natural product-hybridization approach toward anticancer drug discovery: synthesis and antitumor evaluation of CTBC6, designed from sulforaphane and magnolol.
abstract_en: Natural products, with their promising drug-like profiles, inherent pharmacological potential, and a vast reservoir of privileged structures, have long been recognized as a rich source of hit compounds in anticancer drug discovery. Building upon our group's prior work, this study delves into the medicinal chemistry of sulforaphane (SFN)-a star molecule in anticancer research, leading to the discovery of CTBC6 as a novel small molecule anticancer compound. We demonstrated that CTBC6 exhibited potent anti-breast cancer effects both in vitro and in vivo with no significant organ toxicity. We investigated the anti-breast cancer function of the hit hybrid compound CTBC6, and found that CTBC6 induced apoptosis and caused cell cycle arrest at the G2/M phase, while also effectively suppressing cell migration. Moreover, transcriptome analysis and the mechanistic studies revealed that CTBC6 may impede breast cancer progression by inhibiting MEK/ERK signaling pathway. Importantly, the functional targets of CTBC6 in breast cancer was assessed. Through chemical proteomics, we identified CNOT10 and STAT5A as potential direct targets through which CTBC6 exerts its anti-breast cancer activity. Our study lays a good foundation for further medical research of sulforaphane and its derivatives, and also presents a new strategic paradigm for natural product-driven anticancer drug discovery.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些關於健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是在抗癌藥物的開發上。隨著天然產品在藥物發現中的潛力逐漸受到重視，研究者們希望能夠利用這些天然成分來對抗癌症。這不僅能夠幫助我們理解癌症的進展，還可能為未來的治療方法提供新的思路，尤其是針對乳癌這種常見的疾病。透過這些研究，我們或許能找到更有效且副作用較小的治療選擇，進一步提升患者的生活品質。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素及其衍生物在抗癌藥物開發中的潛力，特別是針對乳癌的效果。研究者們關心的是如何利用這些天然化合物來發現新的抗癌藥物，並且希望能夠找到不僅有效而且安全的治療選擇。透過對新化合物CTBC6的研究，團隊希望能夠深入了解其抗癌機制，並評估其在臨床應用中的可行性。
para3: 在實驗方面，研究團隊對CTBC6這個新化合物進行了多方面的測試，包括體外和體內的實驗。他們觀察了CTBC6對乳癌細胞的影響，特別是它如何誘導細胞凋亡和阻止細胞週期進入G2/M階段。此外，研究者們還進行了轉錄組分析，探討CTBC6的作用機制，並利用化學蛋白質組學技術來識別其可能的靶點，這些都是為了更全面地理解CTBC6的抗癌效果。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，CTBC6對乳癌細胞具有顯著的抑制作用，並且在實驗中未觀察到明顯的器官毒性。研究結果指出，CTBC6能夠通過抑制MEK/ERK信號通路來減緩乳癌的進展。此外，研究者們還確認了CNOT10和STAT5A作為CTBC6的潛在靶點，這些發現為未來的醫學研究奠定了良好的基礎，並為天然產品驅動的抗癌藥物發現提供了新的策略。

title_en: Integrated transcriptomic and phenotypic analysis reveals the multi-target antibacterial mechanism of sulforaphane against Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzicola.
abstract_en: Plant pathogenic bacteria represent a significant threat to global crop production, thereby necessitating the development of novel and sustainable bactericides. Sulforaphane (SFN), a natural isothiocyanate derived from cruciferous vegetables, is known to possess diverse biological activities including antimicrobial properties. This study provides a comprehensive evaluation of the antibacterial efficacy and underlying mechanisms of SFN against Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzicola (Xoc), the pathogen responsible for rice bacterial leaf streak. In vitro assays demonstrated that SFN possesses broad-spectrum activity against 12 plant pathogens, with particularly high efficacy against Xoc, as evidenced by an EC₅₀ value of 5.47 μg/mL. In vivo pot experiments indicated that SFN at 100 μg/mL offered both protective (57.91%) and curative (56.70%) control against the disease, surpassing the effectiveness of thiadiazole copper and comparable to that of kasugamycin. Physiological and biochemical analyses revealed that SFN compromised cell membrane integrity, disrupted flagellar structure and motility, and reduced extracellular polysaccharide production and biofilm formation dose-dependently. Integrated transcriptomic and phenotypic analyses revealed that SFN consistently inhibited the oxidative phosphorylation pathway, resulting in reduced ATP levels, increased ROS accumulation, and ultimately impaired energy metabolism and membrane potential. Simultaneously, it downregulated flagellar assembly and associated pathways. These findings indicate that SFN exerts significant antibacterial effects against Xoc through a synergistic multi-target mechanism that involves the disruption of energy metabolism and suppression of virulence. This work not only provides an in-depth understanding of SFN's action mode but also positions it as a highly promising, natural lead compound for developing eco-friendly bactericides.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，蘿蔔硫素作為一種天然化合物，可能成為對抗植物病原菌的新選擇。隨著全球農業面臨越來越多的病害威脅，尋找可持續且有效的防治方法變得尤為重要。這項研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素不僅能有效抑制特定病原菌的生長，還可能成為未來開發環保型農藥的基礎，對於提升作物產量和保障糧食安全具有潛在的貢獻。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於稻米細菌葉斑病病原菌——稻米黃單胞菌的抗菌效果及其機制。研究者關心的問題是如何利用天然物質來對抗這種對農作物造成威脅的病原菌，並希望能找到一種既有效又環保的解決方案。透過對蘿蔔硫素的深入研究，期望能揭示其在植物病害防治中的潛力。
para3: 實驗中，研究者首先進行了體外測試，評估蘿蔔硫素對12種植物病原菌的抗菌活性，特別是針對稻米黃單胞菌的效果。接著，進行了盆栽實驗，使用不同濃度的蘿蔔硫素來觀察其對病害的保護和治療效果。研究者還進行了生理和生化分析，以了解蘿蔔硫素對病原菌的影響機制，並結合轉錄組學和表型分析來探討其作用路徑。
para4: 主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素對稻米黃單胞菌具有顯著的抗菌效果，其EC₅₀值為5.47 μg/mL。在盆栽實驗中，蘿蔔硫素在100 μg/mL的濃度下，對病害提供了57.91%的保護和56.70%的治療效果，超過了某些傳統農藥的效果。研究還發現，蘿蔔硫素能夠破壞病原菌的細胞膜完整性，減少ATP水平，並抑制其能量代謝和毒力表現。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過多重靶點的協同機制來發揮抗菌作用，為未來的環保型農藥開發提供了新的思路。

本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於稻米細菌葉斑病病原菌——稻米黃單胞菌的抗菌效果及其機制。研究者關心的問題是如何利用天然物質來對抗這種對農作物造成威脅的病原菌，並希望能找到一種既有效又環保的解決方案。透過對蘿蔔硫素的深入研究，期望能揭示其在植物病害防治中的潛力。

實驗中，研究者首先進行了體外測試，評估蘿蔔硫素對12種植物病原菌的抗菌活性，特別是針對稻米黃單胞菌的效果。接著，進行了盆栽實驗，使用不同濃度的蘿蔔硫素來觀察其對病害的保護和治療效果。研究者還進行了生理和生化分析，以了解蘿蔔硫素對病原菌的影響機制，並結合轉錄組學和表型分析來探討其作用路徑。

主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素對稻米黃單胞菌具有顯著的抗菌效果，其EC₅₀值為5.47 μg/mL。在盆栽實驗中，蘿蔔硫素在100 μg/mL的濃度下，對病害提供了57.91%的保護和56.70%的治療效果，超過了某些傳統農藥的效果。研究還發現，蘿蔔硫素能夠破壞病原菌的細胞膜完整性，減少ATP水平，並抑制其能量代謝和毒力表現。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過多重靶點的協同機制來發揮抗菌作用，為未來的環保型農藥開發提供了新的思路。

title_en: Sulforaphane-rich aqueous broccoli seed extract suppresses diet-induced obesity via 5-HT2A/AMPK signaling in mice.
journal: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology
abstract_en: Obesity remains a major global health challenge, and current pharmacotherapies have limitations in long-term efficacy, safety, and cost. Broccoli (Brassica oleracea L. var. italica) seeds are a rich source of sulforaphane (SFN), which has demonstrated anti-obesity effects. However, the bioavailability of SFN from broccoli seeds is limited by suboptimal conversion conditions and competing nitrile-formation pathways. This study aims to standardize a high-yield, SFN-rich broccoli seed hydrolysate (BSH), to evaluate its anti-obesity efficacy in mice fed a high-fat diet (HFD), and to elucidate its mechanism via molecular docking and molecular dynamics (MD). SFN hydrolysis was optimized by Liquid chromatography-high-resolution mass spectrometry (LC-HRMS) and High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) across pH, temperature, time, and L-ascorbic acid; 5-hydroxytryptamine receptor 2A (5-HT2A) binding was probed by network pharmacology, molecular docking, and 100 ns MD. HFD-fed male C57BL/6 mice (n = 6 per group) received oral broccoli seed water extract (BWE), BSH, or SFN for 8 weeks. Anti-obesity efficacy was assessed by body/tissue weights, micro-computed tomography (Micro-CT), histology, and serum lipid profiling, while 5-HT2A, adenosine monophosphate-activated protein kinase (AMPK) phosphorylation and other markers in epididymal white adipose tissue (eWAT) and liver were assessed by quantitative real-time PCR (qRT-PCR) and Western blot. SFN production from broccoli seeds was maximized under two hydrolysis regimes: short-term (pH 4 at 35°C for 2 h) and long-term (pH 5 at 25°C for 24 h). In silico analyses predicted stable binding of the sulforaphane-glutathione conjugate (SFN-GSH) to 5-HT2A. In HFD-fed mice, BSH (40-800 mg/kg, p.o.) dose-dependently attenuated HFD-induced body weight gain (800 mg/kg vs HFD, p < 0.001). BWE 400 mg/kg and BSH 40 mg/kg showed broadly comparable effects on body weight. Furthermore, gene and protein analyses in eWAT and liver showed 5-HT2A (Htr2a) suppression, restored AMPK phosphorylation, and downregulation of lipogenic regulators. However, co-administration of the AMPK inhibitor Compound C (5 mg/kg, i.p.) with BSH abolished these BSH-induced effects. Oral administration of a sulforaphane standard (1-10 mg/kg) produced similar effects to BSH (10 mg/kg vs HFD, p < 0.01), suggesting that the anti-obesity effect of BSH is mediated primarily by SFN. In this study, SFN-rich BSH ameliorated obesity in HFD-fed mice via a peripheral 5-HT2A suppression-AMPK activation axis. However, further mechanistic clarification will require studies to more precisely define the role of 5-HT2A in adipocytes.
para1: 肥胖問題在全球健康中仍然是一個重大挑戰，目前的藥物治療在長期效果、安全性和成本上都有其限制。這項研究探討了青花椰菜種子中的蘿蔔硫素，這是一種已被證實具有抗肥胖效果的成分。透過這項研究，我們可能能找到更有效的方式來預防和改善肥胖，進而提升人們的健康狀況，尤其是在飲食不均衡的現代社會中，這樣的研究顯得尤為重要。
para2: 本研究的主題是針對青花椰菜種子中的蘿蔔硫素進行深入探討，特別是它在抗肥胖方面的效果。研究者希望能夠標準化一種高產量的蘿蔔硫素豐富的青花椰菜種子水解物，並評估其在高脂飲食下對小鼠的抗肥胖效果。此外，研究還將探討其作用機制，以期為未來的肥胖治療提供新的思路。
para3: 實驗中使用了雄性C57BL/6小鼠，這些小鼠被餵食高脂飲食，並分為不同組別，接受口服的青花椰菜種子水提取物、青花椰菜種子水解物或蘿蔔硫素，持續8週。研究者通過液相色譜-高解析質譜和高效液相色譜等技術來優化蘿蔔硫素的水解條件，並使用微計算機斷層掃描、組織學和血清脂質分析等方法來評估抗肥胖效果，還對相關基因和蛋白質進行了分析。
para4: 研究結果顯示，青花椰菜種子水解物在高脂飲食的小鼠中能夠有效減少體重增長，並且其效果與蘿蔔硫素的劑量有關。進一步的基因和蛋白質分析顯示，青花椰菜種子水解物能夠抑制5-HT2A的表達，恢復AMPK的磷酸化，並下調脂肪生成調節因子。然而，當與AMPK抑制劑共同使用時，這些效果會被抑制，顯示出AMPK在這一過程中的重要性。總體來看，這項研究表明，蘿蔔硫素豐富的青花椰菜種子水解物可能通過抑制5-HT2A和激活AMPK來改善肥胖問題，但仍需進一步研究以釐清其具體機制。
explanation_zh:
肥胖問題在全球健康中仍然是一個重大挑戰，目前的藥物治療在長期效果、安全性和成本上都有其限制。這項研究探討了青花椰菜種子中的蘿蔔硫素，這是一種已被證實具有抗肥胖效果的成分。透過這項研究，我們可能能找到更有效的方式來預防和改善肥胖，進而提升人們的健康狀況，尤其是在飲食不均衡的現代社會中，這樣的研究顯得尤為重要。

本研究的主題是針對青花椰菜種子中的蘿蔔硫素進行深入探討，特別是它在抗肥胖方面的效果。研究者希望能夠標準化一種高產量的蘿蔔硫素豐富的青花椰菜種子水解物，並評估其在高脂飲食下對小鼠的抗肥胖效果。此外，研究還將探討其作用機制，以期為未來的肥胖治療提供新的思路。

實驗中使用了雄性C57BL/6小鼠，這些小鼠被餵食高脂飲食，並分為不同組別，接受口服的青花椰菜種子水提取物、青花椰菜種子水解物或蘿蔔硫素，持續8週。研究者通過液相色譜-高解析質譜和高效液相色譜等技術來優化蘿蔔硫素的水解條件，並使用微計算機斷層掃描、組織學和血清脂質分析等方法來評估抗肥胖效果，還對相關基因和蛋白質進行了分析。

研究結果顯示，青花椰菜種子水解物在高脂飲食的小鼠中能夠有效減少體重增長，並且其效果與蘿蔔硫素的劑量有關。進一步的基因和蛋白質分析顯示，青花椰菜種子水解物能夠抑制5-HT2A的表達，恢復AMPK的磷酸化，並下調脂肪生成調節因子。然而，當與AMPK抑制劑共同使用時，這些效果會被抑制，顯示出AMPK在這一過程中的重要性。總體來看，這項研究表明，蘿蔔硫素豐富的青花椰菜種子水解物可能通過抑制5-HT2A和激活AMPK來改善肥胖問題，但仍需進一步研究以釐清其具體機制。

title_en: Sulforaphane ameliorates DSS-induced colitis and secondary liver injury in mice: Proposed mechanism in the SCFAs-FFAR2/3-macrophage polarization axis.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SFN), an isothiocyanate derived from cruciferous vegetables, has shown therapeutic potential in inflammatory diseases. Our previous studies demonstrated that SFN ameliorates ulcerative colitis (UC) and restores gut microbiota composition in dextran sulfate sodium (DSS)-induced mice. In the present study, we further investigate the protective effects and underlying mechanisms of SFN against secondary liver injury associated with UC. The results revealed that SFN significantly alleviated pathological damage in both the colon and liver, improved liver function parameters, upregulated intestinal tight junction proteins and Muc2 expression, and inhibited inflammation in DSS-induced colitis mice. Additionally, SFN significantly elevated short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) concentrations, enhanced the expression of SCFA receptors (free fatty acid receptors 2 and 3, FFAR2/3), and modulated macrophage polarization by inhibiting M1 and promoting M2 phenotypes in the colon and liver. Collectively, these findings suggest that SFN may alleviate colitis and secondary liver injury by enhancing intestinal barrier function and reducing inflammatory responses, potentially via the SCFAs-FFAR2/3-macrophage polarization signaling cascade. Thus, SFN may serve as a promising adjunctive therapeutic agent for the prevention and treatment of UC.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些關於健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是在炎症性疾病的管理上。研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能有助於減輕與潰瘍性結腸炎相關的肝臟損傷，這對於改善患者的整體健康狀況是非常重要的。透過增強腸道屏障功能和減少炎症反應，蘿蔔硫素可能成為預防和治療潰瘍性結腸炎的輔助療法，這對於許多面臨這類疾病的人來說，無疑是一個值得期待的發展。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對潰瘍性結腸炎（UC）相關的肝臟二次損傷的保護作用及其潛在機制。研究人員希望了解蘿蔔硫素如何影響腸道和肝臟的健康，特別是在炎症反應和腸道屏障功能方面的影響。這項研究不僅關注潰瘍性結腸炎本身，還延伸到其可能引發的肝臟問題，這使得研究的範圍更加廣泛。
para3: 在實驗中，研究人員使用了誘導潰瘍性結腸炎的小鼠模型，這些小鼠是通過給予硫酸右旋糖酐（DSS）來模擬疾病的。研究團隊觀察了蘿蔔硫素對這些小鼠的影響，包括其對腸道和肝臟的病理損傷、肝功能參數的改善、腸道緊密連接蛋白和Muc2表達的上調，以及炎症的抑制。這些實驗幫助研究人員深入了解蘿蔔硫素的作用機制。
para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能顯著減輕小鼠的腸道和肝臟病理損傷，並改善肝功能指標。此外，蘿蔔硫素還能提高短鏈脂肪酸的濃度，增強短鏈脂肪酸受體的表達，並調節巨噬細胞的極化，抑制M1型並促進M2型的表現。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過增強腸道屏障功能和減少炎症反應，來緩解潰瘍性結腸炎及其引起的肝臟損傷，顯示出其作為輔助治療劑的潛力。

title_en: Sulforaphane improves exercise-induced NRF2 signaling in older adults: an in vivo-ex vivo approach.
abstract_en: Redox signaling is a key mechanism of exercise-induced adaptation. However, studies have demonstrated impaired responses to acute exercise in older organisms. Adjunctive therapies to augment exercise effects may overcome these deficits. Sulforaphane (SFN), a phytochemical from cruciferous vegetables, stimulates NRF2. This study tested the hypothesis that combining acute exercise (in vivo stimulus) with ex vivo SFN treatment would induce greater NRF2 activation and signaling in older adults compared to either treatment alone. Twenty-five older adults (12 men, 13 women; mean age: 67 ± 5 years) performed 30-min cycling exercise (AET). Blood was drawn before and immediately after the AET to isolate PBMCs and incubate with and without SFN (5 µM) treatment (four conditions: DMSO (CON), SFN, exercise (EX), and EX + SFN). PBMCs were harvested after 2-h or 5-h incubation for measures of NRF2 or gene expression for NQO1, HO-1, GR, and GCLC targets, respectively. All treatments (SFN, EX, EX + SFN) increased NRF2 activation compared to CON (p < 0.05). The response to EX + SFN was significantly greater than either SFN or EX alone (2.1-fold versus 1.5-fold, p = 0.01). SFN stimulation resulted in a significant upregulation of all four genes compared to control (p < 0.001). EX + SFN treatment stimulated a greater increase in gene expression compared to EX (p < 0.05); however, SFN did not differ statistically from EX + SFN, suggesting a possible ceiling effect of the SFN concentration in terms of gene expression. There were no significant sex differences in any of the responses. These data suggest that combining exercise with SFN may amplify the strength of NRF2/ARE redox signaling in older adults. ClinicalTrials.gov ID: NCT04848792.

para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，所有的處理組（包括蘿蔔硫素、運動及運動加蘿蔔硫素）都顯著提高了NRF2的活化程度。特別是運動加蘿蔔硫素的組合，其反應強度明顯高於單獨的蘿蔔硫素或運動，顯示出這種組合的潛力。此外，蘿蔔硫素的刺激也顯著上調了與抗氧化相關的四個基因的表達。雖然運動加蘿蔔硫素的組合在基因表達上有更大的提升，但蘿蔔硫素的濃度可能會影響其效果，這需要進一步的研究來確認。

title_en: Sulforaphane Attenuates PM<sub>2.5</sub>-Induced Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease by Modulation of Nrf2 Activating and EGFR/PI3K/AKT Signaling.
abstract_en: This study aimed to investigate the prophylactic and therapeutic effects of Sulforaphane (SFN) on PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and to elucidate the underlying mechanisms. A PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced COPD rat model was established. The protective effects of SFN, administered concurrently with PM<sub>2.5</sub> exposure, were evaluated by analyzing inflammatory cytokine levels and performing histopathological examinations of lung tissues. The antioxidant role of SFN in COPD was further investigated both in vivo and in vitro via the Nrf2 signaling. Subsequently, potential targets and underlying mechanisms of SFN in COPD treatment were predicted through network pharmacological analyses. Molecular docking simulations were then performed to validate the binding affinities between SFN and key target proteins. Furthermore, the modulatory effects of SFN on the EGFR/PI3K/AKT signaling in PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced COPD were investigated both in vivo and in vitro, and EGFR silencing was subsequently used to confirm the involvement of this pathway. Finally, the therapeutic efficacy of SFN in attenuating PM<sub>2.5</sub>-exacerbated COPD progression was evaluated through histopathological examination and assessment of Nrf2 and EGFR/PI3K/AKT pathway activation. SFN significantly attenuated PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced lung injury, inflammation, mucus hypersecretion, and redox stress. Mechanistically, SFN directly activated the Nrf2 signaling, reduced reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation, and thereby mitigated COPD progression. Integrated network pharmacology and molecular docking analyses further identified EGFR as a potential key target of SFN. Consistently, SFN could directly bind to EGFR and suppress EGFR/PI3K/AKT signaling, contributing to its prophylactic effects. Moreover, in the rat AECOPD model, SFN exerted therapeutic benefits primarily via EGFR/PI3K/AKT inhibition, with minimal involvement of Nrf2-driven antioxidant effects. Here, these findings reveal a dual mechanism by which SFN attenuates PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced COPD via Nrf2 activation and inhibition of EGFR/PI3K/AKT signaling, thereby highlighting its potential as a promising candidate for COPD and its acute exacerbation.

title_en: Bench-to-Bedside Evaluation of Sulforaphane/BroccoMax on Fatty Acid Synthesis in Prostate Cancer.
abstract_en: The fatty acid synthesis pathway is a valid target for the prevention of prostate cancer. However, a clinical-grade inhibitor of fatty acid synthesis is still lacking. This bench-to-bedside study was undertaken to determine the feasibility of fatty acid synthesis inhibition using broccoli constituent sulforaphane (SFN) and its clinical-grade formulation BroccoMax (BMAX). Oral administration of SFN to Hi-Myc mice resulted in the inhibition of prostate adenocarcinoma burden by about 61% that was accompanied by a significant decrease in prostate tumor levels of c-Myc and proliferating cell nuclear antigen proteins and increased apoptosis. Expression of acetyl-CoA carboxylase 1 (ACC1) and fatty acid synthase (FASN) was lower by about 46% and 31%, respectively, in the prostate tumor of SFN-treated mice when compared with that of control mice (P < 0.001). Plasma levels of total free fatty acids (TFFA), cholesterol, and total phospholipids were decreased significantly following SFN treatment. In a double-blind clinical trial, patients with histologically confirmed prostate cancer were randomized to the BMAX (n = 19) or placebo (PBO) group (n = 22). Patients were treated with four capsules of BMAX or four capsules of matching PBO orally two times daily after breakfast and dinner for 4 weeks. Prostate tumor expression of c-Myc, ACC1, FASN, and Ki-67 proteins was significantly lower in the BMAX arm when compared with the PBO group. However, the serum or prostate tumor level of acetyl-CoA or TFFA was not decreased by BMAX treatment. A longer duration treatment with BMAX in patients with early-stage prostate cancer may be necessary to lower the circulating or prostate tumor level of TFFA. Fatty acid synthesis is a valid target for the prevention of human prostate cancer. In this study, we determined the feasibility of fatty acid synthesis inhibition using SFN in a mouse model and BMAX, its clinical-grade formulation, in prostatectomy patients.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些對於健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是在前列腺癌的預防方面。研究指出，脂肪酸合成途徑可能是一個有效的目標，透過抑制這個途徑，有望降低前列腺癌的風險。雖然目前尚未有臨床級的脂肪酸合成抑制劑問世，但這項研究探索了使用青花椰菜中的蘿蔔硫素來達成這一目標的可行性，這對於未來的癌症預防策略可能具有重要意義。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討脂肪酸合成的抑制對前列腺癌的影響。研究者關心的問題是，如何利用青花椰菜中的蘿蔔硫素及其臨床級配方BroccoMax來抑制脂肪酸合成，進而影響前列腺癌的發展。這項研究不僅在小鼠模型中進行了初步測試，還在臨床試驗中評估了其對人類患者的影響，這樣的雙重研究設計有助於更全面地理解這一治療策略的潛力。
para3: 實驗的進行分為兩個部分：首先是在Hi-Myc小鼠模型中進行的實驗，這些小鼠被用來模擬前列腺腺癌的情況。研究者給予小鼠口服蘿蔔硫素，觀察其對腫瘤負擔的影響。其次，進行了一項雙盲臨床試驗，招募了確診為前列腺癌的患者，隨機分為接受BroccoMax或安慰劑的兩組，為期四週。這樣的設計可以幫助研究者比較不同治療的效果。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，使用蘿蔔硫素的治療組在前列腺腫瘤的某些蛋白質表達上顯著低於安慰劑組，這可能與腫瘤的增殖和凋亡有關。然而，BroccoMax治療並未顯著降低血清或腫瘤中的某些脂肪酸水平，這表明可能需要更長的治療時間來觀察明顯的效果。總體而言，這項研究支持了脂肪酸合成作為前列腺癌預防的一個有效目標，但仍需進一步的研究來確認其療效。

title_en: Sulforaphane improved cognitive behavior in APP/PS1 mice via promoting structural and functional synaptic plasticity in the hippocampus.
abstract_en: Cognitive dysfunction is the main clinical feature of Alzheimer's disease (AD), and oxidative stress is considered a critical contributor to AD pathogenesis. Sulforaphane (SFN), an aliphatic isothiocyanate predominantly derived from cruciferous vegetables, has been reported to exert antioxidant and neuroprotective effects; however, its impact on synaptic plasticity and the underlying electrophysiological mechanisms in AD remain unclear. In this study, ten-month-old male APPswe/PS1dE9 double transgenic (APP/PS1) mice and wild-type (WT) littermates were randomized to four groups (WT + saline, WT + SFN, APP/PS1 + saline, and APP/PS1 + SFN). SFN was administered at 10 mg kg<sup>-1</sup>via intraperitoneal injection for 30 d in vivo and 1 μM concentration in vitro. Then, we investigated whether SFN improves cognition by restoring hippocampal synaptic plasticity in APP/PS1 mice. It was suggested that SFN administration significantly attenuated Aβ₁₋₄₂-induced oxidative damage in vitro and improved spatial learning and reference memory deficits in APP/PS1 mice. Bioinformatics analysis suggested that SFN modulated synapse-related pathways associated with AD. Consistent with these findings, electrophysiological recordings demonstrated that SFN alleviated hippocampal long-term potentiation (LTP) impairment. Moreover, SFN increased the expression of synaptic proteins PSD-95 and synaptophysin and enhanced dendritic complexity and dendritic spine density in the hippocampal CA1 region, as assessed by immunoblotting and Golgi staining. Together, these results indicated that SFN ameliorated cognitive deficits in AD mice by alleviating LTP inhibition and restoring synaptic structural integrity, supporting a role for synaptic plasticity in the neuroprotective effects of SFN.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在阿茲罕默症的認知功能障礙方面。阿茲罕默症是一種影響記憶和思考能力的疾病，研究顯示氧化壓力可能是導致這種疾病的關鍵因素之一。透過探索蘿蔔硫素的潛在益處，這項研究可能為改善阿茲罕默症患者的認知功能提供新的思路，並且強調了飲食中攝取十字花科蔬菜的重要性，這些蔬菜中含有豐富的蘿蔔硫素，可能對大腦健康有正面影響。
para2: 這項研究主要關注的是蘿蔔硫素對阿茲罕默症小鼠的認知功能及其背後的生理機制。具體來說，研究者希望了解蘿蔔硫素是否能改善阿茲罕默症小鼠的海馬體突觸可塑性，進而影響其認知能力。這個問題的探討不僅有助於理解阿茲罕默症的病理機制，也可能為未來的治療策略提供新的方向。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者使用了十個月大的雙轉基因阿茲罕默症小鼠（APP/PS1）和野生型小鼠作為對照組，將它們隨機分為四組。這些小鼠接受了為期30天的蘿蔔硫素治療，劑量為每公斤10毫克，通過腹腔注射的方式給予。此外，研究者還在體外進行了1微摩爾濃度的實驗，以評估蘿蔔硫素對海馬體突觸可塑性的影響，並觀察其對認知功能的改善效果。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素的施用顯著減少了阿茲罕默症小鼠體內的氧化損傷，並改善了其空間學習和記憶能力。生物信息學分析指出，蘿蔔硫素可能調節了與阿茲罕默症相關的突觸通路。此外，電生理記錄顯示，蘿蔔硫素能夠減輕海馬體長期增強（LTP）的損害，並提高了與突觸相關的蛋白質表達，進一步增強了海馬體CA1區的樹突複雜性和樹突棘密度。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過改善突觸結構的完整性來減輕阿茲罕默症小鼠的認知缺陷。

title_en: Sulforaphane promotes the removal of doxorubicin-induced senescent epithelial and breast cancer cells and activates AhR/CYP1A1-based adaptive response in vitro.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SFN), an isothiocyanate derived from cruciferous vegetables, is characterized by antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties by activating NRF2 pathway, suppressing NFκB pathway, and promoting apoptotic cell death signals and epigenetic changes, respectively. However, SFN-based action against drug-induced senescent cells has never been addressed. In the present study, three breast cancer cell lines with different receptor status, namely triple negative BT-549 and HCC38, and MDA-MB-361 (ER-positive, HER2-positive) along with normal epithelial MCF-10F cells were subjected to 5 μM SFN treatment at both proliferating and non-proliferating (doxorubicin-induced senescence) state. BT-549 proliferating cells were more prone to SFN stimulation than normal ones that was accompanied by oxidative stress-based apoptosis. In contrast, SFN was active in a similar manner against both drug-induced senescent normal and breast cancer cells that resulted in apoptosis-mediated elimination of non-proliferating cells. SFN also stimulated adaptive responses in doxorubicin-induced senescent cells as judged by the activation of aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AhR)/cytochrome P450 1A1 (CYP1A1) pathway and upregulation of heat shock protein 90 (HSP90). In silico analysis with molecular docking also predicted SFN interactions with selected proteins involved in drug detoxification and cell death that warrants further investigation. We postulate that SFN may have senotherapeutic potential, especially against apoptosis resistant chemotherapy-induced senescent normal and breast cancer cells.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些關於健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是在癌症治療方面。蘿蔔硫素是一種來自十字花科蔬菜的化合物，具有抗氧化、抗發炎和抗癌的特性。這些特性可能有助於改善癌症患者的治療效果，尤其是在面對化療引起的細胞老化時。透過這項研究，我們可以更深入了解如何利用蘿蔔硫素來對抗癌症細胞，並可能為未來的治療方法提供新的思路。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於化療引起的細胞老化的影響，特別是針對乳癌細胞的作用。研究者關心的是蘿蔔硫素是否能有效地對抗這些因化療而變得不再增殖的癌細胞，以及它對正常細胞的影響。這項研究的重點在於了解蘿蔔硫素如何影響不同類型的乳癌細胞，並評估其潛在的治療效果。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者選用了三種不同受體狀態的乳癌細胞系，包括三陰性細胞BT-549和HCC38，以及雌激素受體陽性和HER2陽性的MDA-MB-361，還有正常的上皮細胞MCF-10F。這些細胞在增殖和非增殖（由多柔比星誘導的細胞老化）狀態下接受了5μM的蘿蔔硫素處理。研究者觀察了這些細胞在不同處理下的反應，特別是蘿蔔硫素對於細胞死亡的影響。
para4: 主要的發現是，蘿蔔硫素對於增殖中的BT-549細胞刺激效果較正常細胞明顯，並伴隨著氧化壓力引起的細胞死亡。而在非增殖的細胞中，無論是正常細胞還是乳癌細胞，蘿蔔硫素都能有效促進細胞死亡。此外，蘿蔔硫素還能激活與細胞適應性反應相關的路徑，顯示出其在對抗化療引起的細胞老化方面的潛力。這些結果提示蘿蔔硫素可能在未來的治療中具有重要的應用價值。

title_en: Restoration of Immune Surveillance in Prostate Cancer Prevention by Sulforaphane in Hi-Myc Mice.
abstract_en: Oral administration of broccoli constituent sulforaphane (SFN) prevents prostate cancer development in preclinical mouse models. However, the mechanism(s) underlying prostate cancer prevention by SFN are not fully understood. In this study, we used a human relevant mouse model (Hi-Myc mice) to demonstrate restoration of immune surveillance by oral SFN administration. Treatment of Hi-Myc mice with SFN for 16 weeks resulted in about 1.33-fold increase in the number of prostate tumor-infiltrating CD8α + T cells (p = 0.02 by Student's t-test). The number of CD4+ helper T cells was not affected by SFN treatment. The number of CD11c/MHCII+ dendritic cells was increased by about 57% upon SFN administration. On the other hand, the number of NKp46+ natural killer cells was not significantly affected by SFN treatment. Oral administration of SFN resulted in about 30% decrease in the number of Gr1/CD11b+ myeloid-derived suppressor cells in the prostate tumor when compared to control mice. Plasma levels of interleukin (IL)-1α, IL-1β, IL-4, IL-5, IL-10, and C-X-C motif chemokine ligand 2 (CXCL2 or MIP-2) were statistically significantly lower in SFN-treated mice when compared to control mice. Treatment of recurrent prostate cancer patients with 200 μmol/day of SFN-rich broccoli sprout extract for 20 weeks also caused a statistically significant decrease in plasma levels of IL-1β, IL-4, and IL-13. Cell proliferation inhibition by SFN in vitro was partially but significantly attenuated by IL-4 and IL-13 supplementation in 22Rv1 cells. These results indicate restoration of immune surveillance by oral SFN treatment in Hi-Myc mouse model.

title_en: Bioaccessible sulforaphane attenuates oxidative stress-driven parainflammation in intestinal epithelial cells.
abstract_en: The dietary consumption of plant-based foods rich in sulforaphane (SFN) and its precursor, glucoraphanin, contributes to health due to SFN's ability to mitigate inflammation. Based on this premise, the present study aims to demonstrate the capacity of bioaccessible breakdown compounds from glucosinolate (SFN) obtained from a broccoli stalk-based ingredient to prevent parainflammatory syndrome associated with oxidative stress, which is strongly linked with a range of metabolic diseases and ageing. UHPLC-ESI-QqQ-MS/MS-based analysis revealed SFN as the only organosulfur compound at a quantifiable concentration in the bioaccessible fraction (4.32 mg per kg dw, corresponding to 0.072 µg mL-1 of SFN). The assessment of SFN on the in vitro ability to decrease cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) concentration and modulate oxidative stress (8-iso-PGF2α) and inflammatory markers (PGF2α and PGE2) evidenced a significant anti-inflammatory capacity, which is, to some extent, independent of the weaker capacity to prevent oxidative stress. This result was further confirmed by implementing a model system, which analysed COX-2 expression and the synthesis of isoprostanoids at decreasing concentrations of SFN, starting at the bioaccessible level. The main results obtained emphasise the value of broccoli stalks as a source of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds at operative concentrations in the intestinal lumen and encourage their consideration as a valuable ingredient for health-promoting co-products.

title_en: The H<sub>2</sub>S donor sulforaphane inhibits NLRP<sub>3</sub> inflammasome activation by inducing mitochondrial autophagy and mitigating CBS-H<sub>2</sub>S axis damage in in-vitro and in-vivo models of Parkinson's disease.
abstract_en: Hydrogen sulfide (H₂S) plays a crucial neuroprotective role in Parkinson's disease (PD). Cystathionine-β-synthase (CBS), a key enzyme involved in H₂S biosynthesis, exhibits expression deficiencies that are closely linked to PD progression. This suggests that enhancing the CBS-H₂S signaling axis to restore H₂S homeostasis may be a critical approach for preventing and treating PD. In the present study, the H₂S donor sulforaphane (SFN) was investigated to elucidate its neuroprotective mechanisms through activation of the CBS-H₂S axis. siRNA-mediated silencing of Nrf2 and CBS was employed to clarify the role of each in SFN's effects. Our findings demonstrate that SFN promotes CBS expression and H₂S synthesis, activates mitophagy to clear damaged mitochondria, reduces mitochondrial-derived reactive oxygen species (mtROS) levels, and inhibits the activation of NLRP3 inflammasomes and caspase-1. In the MPTP-induced PD mouse model, SFN improved motor performance, increased the survival rate of tyrosine hydroxylase (TH)-positive dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra, and restored dopamine metabolism in the striatum, normalizing the DOPAC/DA and 5-HIAA/5-HT ratios. Electron microscopy revealed that SFN facilitated the clearance of damaged mitochondria through autophagosomes and blocked mtROS-mediated NLRP3 inflammasome activation. In the MPP<sup>+</sup>-induced BV-2 microglial cell model, SFN upregulated CBS expression, enhanced H₂S synthesis, increased the LC3-II/I ratio, and inhibited p62 degradation, thereby promoting the recovery of mitochondrial membrane potential and reducing ROS release. These effects were still observed under Nrf2 silencing conditions, indicating that SFN's neuroprotective effects are mediated through the CBS-H₂S axis independently of Nrf2 signaling. Collectively, these findings indicate that SFN reshapes the CBS-H₂S signaling axis, activates mitochondrial autophagy, and suppresses inflammation, offering novel insights into multi-target therapeutic approaches for PD and underscoring the essential role of H₂S in neuroprotection.
para1: 氫硫化物（H₂S）在帕金森病的神經保護中扮演著重要角色。研究顯示，與帕金森病進展密切相關的酵素——半胱氨酸β合成酶（CBS）表現不足，這意味著提升CBS-H₂S信號通路以恢復H₂S的平衡，可能是預防和治療帕金森病的一個關鍵方法。因此，了解如何增強這一信號通路，對於改善帕金森病患者的健康狀況具有潛在的啟示。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素（SFN）作為H₂S供體的神經保護機制，特別是它如何通過激活CBS-H₂S通路來發揮作用。研究者關注的是SFN對CBS表達和H₂S合成的影響，以及這些變化如何影響帕金森病的病理過程，這對於理解帕金森病的治療策略具有重要意義。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了小鼠模型來模擬MPTP誘導的帕金森病，並且通過siRNA技術抑制Nrf2和CBS的表達，以澄清它們在SFN作用中的角色。這樣的設計使得研究者能夠深入了解SFN如何促進CBS的表達和H₂S的合成，並探討其對線粒體功能和炎症反應的影響。
para4: 主要發現顯示，SFN能夠促進CBS的表達和H₂S的合成，並激活線粒體自噬，清除受損的線粒體，降低線粒體衍生的活性氧（mtROS）水平。此外，SFN還能抑制NLRP3炎症小體和caspase-1的活化。在小鼠模型中，SFN改善了運動表現，增加了多巴胺能神經元的存活率，並恢復了多巴胺的代謝。這些結果顯示，SFN通過重塑CBS-H₂S信號通路，激活線粒體自噬，並抑制炎症，為帕金森病的多靶點治療提供了新見解。

title_en: Integrative Bioinformatic Identification and Molecular Docking of Quercetin and Sulforaphane-Associated Prognostic Targets in Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma.
abstract_en: Pancreatic adenocarcinoma (PAAD) remains a highly lethal malignancy with limited therapeutic options, motivating the search for robust prognostic markers and tractable therapeutic targets. In this study, we applied an integrative bioinformatic pipeline combining cross-cohort differential expression analysis, high-confidence protein-protein interaction network reconstruction, and topological hub-gene prioritization. Hub candidates were then intersected with curated target repertoires of multi-target chemicals (notably quercetin and sulforaphane [SFN]) to nominate pharmacologically accessible "elite" targets. Downstream in silico validation included comparative mRNA and protein expression profiling, correlations with immune infiltration metrics, survival prognostic assessments, and molecular docking to evaluate ligand-target complementarity. This multilayered approach consistently highlighted extracellular matrix remodeling, integrin-mediated adhesion, and pericellular proteolysis as central processes in PAAD biology and identified COL1A1, ITGA2, and PLAU as top-priority targets that combine high network centrality with overlap to phytochemical target spaces. These genes demonstrated tumor-enriched expression, adverse survival associations, and distinct immune-microenvironment correlations, suggesting a potential involvement in pro-tumorigenic remodeling processes. Molecular docking analyses suggested computationally feasible ligand-target binding hypotheses, with quercetin exhibiting comparatively stronger predicted affinities than SFN across all targets.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防有重要的啟示，特別是在胰臟腺癌這種致命性癌症的治療上。由於目前對於這種癌症的治療選擇有限，因此尋找新的預後標記和治療目標變得相當重要。研究中提到的幾個基因可能成為未來治療的關鍵，這些基因的表現與腫瘤的發展和患者的生存率有關，這意味著透過這些標記，我們或許能更好地理解和預測疾病的進展，並為患者提供更有效的治療方案。
para2: 本研究的主題是針對胰臟腺癌（PAAD）的生物信息學分析，旨在尋找與疾病相關的預後標記和治療目標。研究者們關注的是如何利用不同的數據分析技術來識別出在胰臟腺癌中表現異常的基因，並探索這些基因在腫瘤生物學中的角色。透過這些分析，研究者希望能夠找到能夠改善患者預後的生物標記，並為未來的治療提供新的方向。
para3: 這項研究採用了綜合的生物信息學方法，結合了多個數據集的差異表達分析、蛋白質互作網絡的重建，以及關鍵基因的優先排序。研究者首先識別出一些重要的基因，然後將這些基因與已知的多靶點化合物（如槲皮素和蘿蔔硫素）的靶標進行交集分析。接著，研究者進行了多層次的計算驗證，包括比較mRNA和蛋白質的表達、與免疫浸潤的相關性、預後評估以及分子對接，以評估配體與靶標的相容性。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，研究中突出的基因如COL1A1、ITGA2和PLAU在胰臟腺癌中表現增強，並且與不良的生存預後有關。這些基因在腫瘤微環境中也顯示出獨特的相關性，可能參與促進腫瘤的重塑過程。此外，分子對接分析表明，槲皮素對這些靶標的結合能力預測優於蘿蔔硫素，這為未來的藥物開發提供了有價值的線索。

title_en: Sulforaphane Attenuates Methamphetamine-Induced Neurotoxicity via Activation of the PI3K/AKT Pathway.
journal: Journal of biochemical and molecular toxicology
abstract_en: Methamphetamine (METH) is a widely abused psychostimulant known to induce neurotoxicity through oxidative stress and apoptosis. Sulforaphane (SFN), a natural compound found in cruciferous vegetables, has shown neuroprotective potential, but its role in METH-induced neuronal injury remains unclear. In this study, we integrated network pharmacology together with in vitro and in vivo experiments to investigate the protective effects of SFN and its underlying mechanisms. A total of 235 overlapping targets were identified between SFN and METH-related neurotoxicity, with significant enrichment in apoptosis and the PI3K/AKT signaling pathway. Molecular docking suggested a potential interaction between SFN and AKT1 with favorable binding affinity. In HT22 hippocampal neuronal cells, SFN significantly improved cell viability, restored the Bcl-2/BAX ratio, inhibited caspase-3 activation, and reduced nuclear damage caused by METH. These protective effects were associated with increased phosphorylation of PI3K and AKT, and were markedly diminished by the PI3K inhibitor LY294002. In a METH-induced neurotoxicity mouse model, SFN ameliorated hippocampal histopathological damage, reduced TUNEL-positive apoptotic cells, and normalized the expression of BAX, Bcl-2, cleaved caspase-3, and phosphorylated PI3K/AKT. These findings suggest that SFN alleviates METH-induced neurotoxicity by activating the PI3K/AKT pathway and inhibiting mitochondria-dependent apoptosis, providing evidence for its potential as a therapeutic candidate for METH-related neuronal injury.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在面對毒品濫用的問題上。甲基安非他命（METH）是一種常見的精神刺激劑，會對神經系統造成傷害，導致氧化壓力和細胞凋亡。研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素這種天然化合物可能對神經系統有保護作用，這對於尋找減少毒品對神經系統影響的策略來說，具有重要意義。透過這項研究，我們可以更深入了解如何利用天然成分來對抗由毒品引起的神經損傷，這對於未來的健康促進和疾病預防工作都可能有所幫助。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在甲基安非他命引起的神經損傷中的保護作用及其機制。研究者關心的是，蘿蔔硫素是否能夠減輕由METH引起的神經毒性，並且希望了解其背後的生物學機制。這不僅涉及到藥物的作用，還包括細胞層面的變化，特別是與細胞凋亡和信號傳導路徑相關的變化。透過這些研究，科學家希望能夠找到有效的治療方法來應對這類神經損傷。
para3: 在實驗設計上，研究者結合了網絡藥理學以及體外和體內的實驗來進行研究。他們首先識別了235個與蘿蔔硫素和METH相關的重疊靶點，並發現這些靶點在細胞凋亡和PI3K/AKT信號通路中有顯著的富集。接著，在HT22海馬神經細胞中，研究者觀察到蘿蔔硫素能顯著改善細胞活性，並恢復Bcl-2/BAX比例，抑制caspase-3的活化，減少METH造成的核損傷。此外，研究者還在METH誘導的神經毒性小鼠模型中進行了實驗，以評估蘿蔔硫素的保護效果。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能夠減輕METH引起的神經毒性，這是通過激活PI3K/AKT通路和抑制依賴於線粒體的細胞凋亡來實現的。在細胞實驗中，蘿蔔硫素改善了細胞的生存率，並且在小鼠模型中減少了海馬區的組織損傷和凋亡細胞的數量。這些結果提供了蘿蔔硫素作為治療METH相關神經損傷的潛在候選藥物的證據，但仍需進一步研究來確認其具體的療效和機制。

title_en: Mining of key genes involved in the sulforaphane biosynthetic pathway of moringa and cloning, expression, and functional verification of MoMYR1.
abstract_en: Moringa oleifera, a plant of significant medicinal and edible value, is renowned for its bioactive compound isothiocyanates (ITCs) with multiple health benefits. This study investigates the biosynthetic mechanism of ITCs in M. oleifera. Local BLAST alignment and KEGG pathway analysis of the Moringa genome identified nine key genes encoding six enzymes-BCAT, CYP79F1, CYP83A1, UGT, SOT, and MYR-potentially involved in ITCs biosynthesis. Expression correlation analysis showed that only myrosinase genes MoMYR1 and MoMYR3 exhibited significant positive correlation with sulforaphane content across tissues (p < 0.05). The MoMYR1 gene (GeneBank: PP738617) was successfully cloned for the first time. Heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21 produced recombinant MoMYR1 protein, while expression in Pichia pastoris GS115 yielded no detectable MoMYR3 protein. Molecular docking revealed a binding energy of -8.0 kcal/mol between the protein and substrate, indicating strong affinity. In vitro enzymatic assays demonstrated that recombinant MoMYR1 catalyzed Sinigrin and GL with specific activities of 0.2565 and 0.2447 μmol·(min·mg)<sup>-1</sup>, and catalytic efficiencies of 30.17 and 28.79 U·g<sup>-1</sup>, respectively. LC-MS analysis confirmed the conversion of GL to sulforaphane by heterologously expressed MoMYR1. This study identifies MoMYR1 as a key enzyme gene in ITCs biosynthesis in Moringa oleifera, providing a theoretical foundation and critical target for elucidating regulatory mechanisms and developing green production strategies.

title_en: Sulforaphane attenuates high-fat diet-induced myocardial injury via AMPK/SIRT1/P65 signaling in mice.
abstract_en: Obesity-induced metabolic disorders are closely associated with myocardial injury, in which lipid accumulation and chronic inflammation play critical roles. Activation of the AMPK/SIRT1 pathway has been reported to suppress NF-κB-mediated inflammatory responses and protect cardiac function. Sulforaphane (SFN), a bioactive isothiocyanate derived from cruciferous vegetables, exhibits potent metabolic and anti-inflammatory properties; however, its protective effects and underlying mechanisms in obesity-related myocardial injury remain incompletely understood. This study aimed to investigate whether SFN alleviates high-fat diet (HFD)-induced myocardial injury and to elucidate the involvement of the AMPK/SIRT1/NF-κB signaling pathway. Male C57BL/6 mice were fed a normal diet or HFD, with or without SFN treatment, for 16 weeks. Metabolic parameters, serum lipid profiles, myocardial injury markers, inflammatory cytokines and histopathological changes were evaluated. In vitro, palmitic acid-treated H9c2 cardiomyocytes were used to assess the mechanistic role of AMPK/SIRT1 signaling, with pharmacological inhibition of AMPK. SFN significantly reduced body weight, fasting glucose, serum triglycerides, total cholesterol, free fatty acids, and LDL levels in HFD-fed mice. SFN markedly attenuated myocardial structural damage and decreased serum CK, CK-MB, and cTnI levels. Inflammatory cytokines (TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6) were suppressed at both serum and myocardial mRNA levels. Mechanistically, SFN enhanced AMPK phosphorylation and SIRT1 expression while inhibiting p65 phosphorylation. In vitro, AMPK inhibition reversed the anti-inflammatory effects of SFN. SFN effectively alleviates HFD-induced myocardial injury by activating the AMPK/SIRT1 pathway and suppressing NF-κB-mediated inflammation, highlighting its potential as a therapeutic agent for obesity-related cardiac injury.

title_en: Sulforaphane attenuates HFD-induced enteritis in yellow river carp by restoring immune homeostasis, repairing intestinal barrier, and modulating gut microbiota.
abstract_en: High-fat diets (HFD) exacerbate intestinal health risks in aquaculture. This study evaluated effects of dietary sulforaphane (SFN) supplementation on intestinal morphology, biochemistry, microbiota, and metabolism in Yellow River carp (Cyprinus carpio haematopterus) fed HFD. Five isoprotein diets diets were formulated: control (CN, 6 % lipid), HFD (12.29 % lipid), and HFD with 10 (HS10), 15 (HS15), or 20 (HS20) mg/kg SFN. Results demonstrated that HFD induced marked intestinal inflammation featuring villi deformation, oxidative stress, epithelial exfoliation, mitochondrial swelling, significantly elevated pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-α/IL-1β/IL-6; p < 0.05), and downregulated tight junction protein genes (Occludin/ZO-1/Claudin-3; p < 0.05), concomitant with microbial dysbiosis (Proteobacteria dominance>70 %, reduced Cetobacterium) and disrupted metabolic pathways (amino acid imbalance). SFN supplementation significantly enhanced antioxidant enzyme activities (SOD/CAT/GSH-Px, p < 0.05), peaking in HS15, which also exhibited minimized pro-inflammatory cytokines and maximized tight junction expression (p < 0.05). Furthermore, 16S rRNA gene sequencing of the gut microbiota indicated that SFN restored microbial homeostasis, notably by increasing the abundance of Cetobacterium. Metabolomic analysis based on KEGG enrichment revealed that these beneficial effects were associated with the activation of FoxO signaling, enhanced lysosomal function, and upregulation of coenzyme A biosynthesis. Critically, HS15 demonstrated optimal efficacy across all parameters. Therefore, 15 mg/kg SFN is identified as the optimal dose to ameliorate intestinal health in Yellow River carp, providing a theoretical basis for functional aquafeed development.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在水產養殖領域。高脂飲食可能會對魚類的腸道健康造成負面影響，導致炎症和其他健康問題。透過補充蘿蔔硫素，研究顯示可以改善腸道的健康狀況，這對於養殖業者來說，可能是一個重要的發現，因為這不僅能提高魚類的生長率，還能減少疾病的風險，進而提升整體養殖效益。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於高脂飲食下的黃河鯉魚腸道健康的影響。研究者關心的問題是，當魚類攝取高脂飲食時，腸道會出現哪些健康問題，以及補充蘿蔔硫素是否能改善這些情況。這項研究不僅關注腸道的形態學變化，還包括生化指標、微生物群落的變化和新陳代謝的影響，試圖全面了解蘿蔔硫素的潛在益處。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了五種不同的飲食配方，分別為對照組（含6%脂肪）、高脂飲食組（含12.29%脂肪），以及三個不同劑量的蘿蔔硫素補充組（10、15和20 mg/kg）。研究對象為黃河鯉魚，透過觀察其腸道的形態變化、炎症指標、微生物組成及代謝途徑，來評估蘿蔔硫素的效果。這樣的設計使得研究能夠全面分析不同飲食對魚類健康的影響。
para4: 主要發現顯示，高脂飲食會導致腸道炎症、氧化壓力和微生物失調，而補充蘿蔔硫素則能顯著改善這些情況。特別是在15 mg/kg的劑量下，蘿蔔硫素不僅提高了抗氧化酶的活性，還減少了炎症指標，並促進了腸道緊密連接蛋白的表達。此外，微生物組的分析顯示，蘿蔔硫素能恢復腸道的微生物平衡，這些結果為水產養殖中功能性飼料的開發提供了理論基礎。

title_en: Bench-to-Bedside Evaluation of Sulforaphane/BroccoMax® on Fatty Acid Synthesis in Prostate Cancer.
abstract_en: Fatty acid synthesis pathway is a valid target for prevention of prostate cancer. However, a clinical grade inhibitor of fatty acid synthesis is still lacking. This bench-to-bedside study was undertaken to determine the feasibility of fatty acid synthesis inhibition using broccoli constituent sulforaphane (SFN) and its clinical grade formulation BroccoMax® (BMAX). Oral administration of SFN to Hi-Myc mice resulted in inhibition of prostate adenocarcinoma burden by about 61% that was accompanied by a significant decrease in prostate tumor levels of c-Myc and PCNA proteins and increased apoptosis. Expression of acetyl-CoA carboxylase 1 (ACC1) and fatty acid synthase (FASN) were lower by about 46% and 31%, respectively, in the prostate tumor of SFN-treated mice when compared to that of control mice (P < 0.001). Plasma levels of total free fatty acids (TFFA), cholesterol, and total phospholipids were decreased significantly following SFN treatment. In a double-blind clinical trial, patients with histologically confirmed prostate cancer were randomized to the BMAX group (n= 19) or placebo group (n= 22). Patients were treated with 4 capsules BMAX or 4 capsules of matching placebo orally two times daily after breakfast and dinner for 4 weeks. Prostate tumor expression of c-Myc, ACC1, FASN, and Ki-67 proteins were significantly lower in the BMAX arm when compared to PBO group. However, serum or prostate tumor level of acetyl-CoA or TFFA was not decreased by BMAX treatment. A longer duration treatment with BMAX in early-stage prostate cancer patients may be necessary to lower circulating or prostate tumor level of TFFA.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些有關預防前列腺癌的健康啟示。研究指出，脂肪酸合成途徑可能是預防前列腺癌的一個有效目標。雖然目前還缺乏臨床級的脂肪酸合成抑制劑，但這項研究探索了使用青花椰菜中的成分蘿蔔硫素來抑制脂肪酸合成的可行性，這可能為未來的癌症預防策略提供新的方向。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在前列腺癌預防中的潛力。研究者關注的是如何利用青花椰菜中的這種成分來抑制脂肪酸合成，進而減少前列腺腺癌的負擔。研究中提到的脂肪酸合成途徑與前列腺癌的發展有關，因此了解這一過程對於開發新的治療方法至關重要。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者首先使用了Hi-Myc小鼠作為模型，這些小鼠被用來模擬前列腺癌的發展。研究者給予小鼠口服蘿蔔硫素，結果顯示小鼠的前列腺腺癌負擔減少了約61%。此外，研究者還進行了一項雙盲臨床試驗，將確診為前列腺癌的患者隨機分為兩組，一組接受青花椰菜成分的治療，另一組則接受安慰劑，為期四週。
para4: 主要發現顯示，接受青花椰菜成分治療的患者，其前列腺腫瘤中某些蛋白質的表達顯著降低，這可能與腫瘤的生長有關。然而，治療後患者的血清或前列腺腫瘤中的某些脂肪酸水平並未顯著下降。這表明，對於早期前列腺癌患者，可能需要更長時間的治療才能有效降低這些脂肪酸的水平。

title_en: Dietary sulforaphane glucosinolate alleviates stress-induced depression and demyelination through gut-brain axis modulation.
abstract_en: Chronic stress is a major risk factor for depression and white matter injury, yet effective preventive strategies remain limited. Here, we investigated whether an 8-week diet supplemented with 0.1% sulforaphane glucosinolate (SGS) could prevent depression-like behaviors and corpus callosum (CC) demyelination in C57BL/6 mice exposed to 14 d of chronic restraint stress (CRS). SGS pre-treatment significantly reduced immobility in the forced swim test and showed a trend toward preserving sucrose preference compared with stressed controls. Consistently, Black-Gold II staining and myelin basic protein (MBP) immunofluorescence demonstrated that SGS markedly attenuated CRS-induced myelin loss in the corpus callosum. Gut microbiota profiling by 16S rRNA sequencing revealed unchanged α-diversity but clear β-diversity shifts, including enrichment of taxa such as Lacrimispora and Roseburia in SGS-fed mice under CRS. Untargeted plasma metabolomics further showed that SGS normalized stress-elevated metabolites, and correlation analyses linked specific microbial taxa and metabolites to myelin integrity. Together, these findings indicate that dietary SGS confers stress resilience and preserves white matter integrity, at least in part, through modulation of the gut-brain axis. SGS therefore represents a promising nutraceutical strategy for the prevention of stress-related neuropsychiatric and white matter disorders.
para1: 慢性壓力是導致憂鬱症和白質損傷的重要風險因素，但目前有效的預防策略仍然有限。這項研究探討了在飲食中添加蘿蔔硫素是否能幫助預防與壓力相關的憂鬱行為和白質損傷。這對於尋找新的健康飲食方式來減少壓力影響，可能會有啟示，尤其是在現代社會中，許多人都面臨著各種壓力源。透過改善飲食，或許能在一定程度上增強我們的心理健康和神經系統的穩定性。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於慢性壓力引起的憂鬱行為和白質損傷的預防效果。研究者關心的是，透過飲食補充蘿蔔硫素，是否能減少因為慢性壓力而產生的負面影響，特別是在小鼠模型中觀察這些效果。這樣的研究不僅有助於了解壓力對大腦的影響，也可能為未來的飲食干預提供科學依據。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了C57BL/6小鼠作為實驗對象，並將它們分為兩組：一組在為期8週的飲食中添加0.1%的蘿蔔硫素，另一組則作為對照組，沒有添加。所有小鼠都經歷了14天的慢性約束壓力，研究者接著使用不同的測試方法來評估小鼠的行為變化和大腦白質的健康狀況，包括強迫游泳測試和組織學分析等，以觀察蘿蔔硫素的影響。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素的預處理顯著減少了小鼠在強迫游泳測試中的靜止時間，並且在保持糖水偏好方面也有改善的趨勢。此外，組織學分析顯示，蘿蔔硫素能有效減少慢性壓力引起的白質損失。腸道微生物群的分析顯示，雖然α多樣性沒有變化，但β多樣性卻有明顯的變化，這表明蘿蔔硫素可能透過調節腸腦軸來增強對壓力的抵抗力，並維持白質的完整性。這些結果暗示，蘿蔔硫素可能成為預防與壓力相關的神經精神疾病和白質疾病的有前景的營養策略。

title_en: Magnetic field-assisted blanching enhances sulforaphane synthesis in broccoli florets, revealed by UPLC-ESI-QTRAP-MS/MS.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SF) is generated from glucoraphanin hydrolysis catalyzed by endogenous myrosinase in Brassicaceae vegetables such as broccoli, with blanching critical to maximize SF formation. However, the effect of magnetic field (MF) treatment on glucoraphanin hydrolysis remains unclear. This study investigated MF-assisted blanching of broccoli florets under 0-10 mT, monitoring myrosinase activity and quantifying SF, sulforaphane nitrile (SFN), and precursor glucosinolates using UPLC-ESI-QTRAP-MS/MS. MF-assisted blanching significantly enhanced myrosinase activity and SF formation, with the greatest effect at 1.0 mT, where myrosinase activity increased 3.11-fold and SF content reached 61.14 ± 1.21 μmol/100 g fresh weight, a 1.89-fold increase relative to the control (blanching only). SFN increased but remained at trace levels, reaching 3.10 ± 0.10 μmol/100 g fresh weight at 1.0 mT versus 0.24 ± 0.09 μmol/100 g in the control. Enhanced hydrolysis was supported by residual glucosinolate levels. These findings suggest a promising strategy to improve SF content in broccoli.
para1: 這項研究對健康或疾病預防的啟示在於，青花椰菜中的蘿蔔硫素可能對我們的健康有益，而這種物質的生成可以透過特定的處理方法來增強。蘿蔔硫素被認為具有抗氧化和抗炎的特性，可能有助於降低某些疾病的風險。因此，了解如何有效地提高青花椰菜中的蘿蔔硫素含量，對於促進健康和預防疾病是非常重要的。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討磁場處理對青花椰菜中蘿蔔硫素生成的影響。研究者關心的是，如何透過不同強度的磁場來促進青花椰菜中蘿蔔硫素的形成，並且希望能夠找到最佳的處理條件，以提高這種有益成分的含量。這樣的研究不僅有助於了解青花椰菜的營養價值，也可能對食品加工技術有實際應用的意義。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者對青花椰菜的花蕾進行了磁場輔助的燙煮處理，磁場強度範圍從0到10毫特斯拉不等。研究團隊監測了青花椰菜中酵素的活性，並使用高效液相色譜質譜聯用技術來量化蘿蔔硫素、蘿蔔硫素腈及其前驅物的含量。這樣的實驗設計使得研究者能夠清楚地了解不同處理條件下，青花椰菜中蘿蔔硫素的生成情況。
para4: 研究結果顯示，磁場輔助的燙煮處理確實顯著提高了青花椰菜中酵素的活性和蘿蔔硫素的生成，尤其在1.0毫特斯拉的磁場強度下，酵素活性提高了3.11倍，蘿蔔硫素的含量達到61.14微摩爾每100克新鮮重量，較對照組增加了1.89倍。雖然蘿蔔硫素腈的含量也有所增加，但仍然處於微量水平。這些發現為提高青花椰菜中蘿蔔硫素的含量提供了一個有前景的策略。

title_en: Sulforaphane in Cancer Prevention and Therapy: A State-of-the-Art Review of Epidemiological Evidence, Molecular Mechanisms, and Translational Challenges.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SFN), an aliphatic isothiocyanate derived from cruciferous vegetables such as broccoli, has emerged as a chemopreventive dietary agent. SFN exerts multifaceted anticancer effects through the activation of the nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2)-antioxidant response element (ARE) pathways, inhibition of histone deacetylases (HDACs) and hypoxia-inducible factor-1α (HIF-1α), and regulation of apoptosis and autophagy. Epidemiological studies have consistently associated cruciferous vegetable intake with reduced cancer risk, while mechanistic research has elucidated the capacity of SFN to modulate redox balance, detoxification pathways, and epigenetic processes. Recent clinical trials have further demonstrated its potential to reduce carcinogenic biomarker levels and support metabolic detoxification. This review integrates evidence from epidemiological observations, molecular mechanisms, and clinical studies to provide a comprehensive understanding of the role of SFN in cancer prevention and therapy. Finally, translational challenges, including limited bioavailability, dose optimization, and standardization of broccoli-derived preparations, are discussed as critical factors for successfully translating SFN therapies from bench to bedside.
para1: 蘿蔔硫素是一種來自青花椰菜等十字花科蔬菜的化合物，近年來被認為對健康有潛在的保護作用，特別是在癌症預防方面。許多流行病學研究顯示，經常食用這類蔬菜的人，罹患癌症的風險較低。這些研究結果讓我們意識到，透過飲食調整，可能有助於降低某些疾病的發生率，進而促進整體健康。雖然這些發現令人鼓舞，但仍需進一步的研究來確認蘿蔔硫素的具體效果及其在日常飲食中的應用。
para2: 這項研究主要關注蘿蔔硫素在癌症預防和治療中的作用。研究者們希望了解這種化合物如何透過不同的生物機制來影響癌症的發展。具體來說，研究探討了蘿蔔硫素如何啟動抗氧化反應、抑制某些酶的活性，以及調節細胞的死亡和自噬過程。這些機制的深入了解，將有助於我們更好地掌握蘿蔔硫素在抗癌方面的潛力。
para3: 研究團隊進行了一系列的實驗，包括流行病學觀察、分子機制的探討以及臨床試驗。他們分析了食用青花椰菜和其他十字花科蔬菜與癌症風險之間的關聯，並進一步研究蘿蔔硫素如何影響細胞內的生化反應。臨床試驗的部分則評估了蘿蔔硫素對癌症生物標記物的影響，並探討其在代謝解毒過程中的潛在作用，這些研究為未來的應用提供了重要的科學依據。
para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能有助於降低某些致癌生物標記物的水平，並支持身體的代謝解毒過程。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素在癌症預防和治療中可能具有一定的潛力，但仍需進一步的研究來確認其效果。此外，研究也指出了在將蘿蔔硫素的療法從實驗室轉向臨床應用時所面臨的挑戰，例如生物利用度的限制、劑量的優化以及青花椰菜衍生產品的標準化等問題。
explanation_zh:
蘿蔔硫素是一種來自青花椰菜等十字花科蔬菜的化合物，近年來被認為對健康有潛在的保護作用，特別是在癌症預防方面。許多流行病學研究顯示，經常食用這類蔬菜的人，罹患癌症的風險較低。這些研究結果讓我們意識到，透過飲食調整，可能有助於降低某些疾病的發生率，進而促進整體健康。雖然這些發現令人鼓舞，但仍需進一步的研究來確認蘿蔔硫素的具體效果及其在日常飲食中的應用。

這項研究主要關注蘿蔔硫素在癌症預防和治療中的作用。研究者們希望了解這種化合物如何透過不同的生物機制來影響癌症的發展。具體來說，研究探討了蘿蔔硫素如何啟動抗氧化反應、抑制某些酶的活性，以及調節細胞的死亡和自噬過程。這些機制的深入了解，將有助於我們更好地掌握蘿蔔硫素在抗癌方面的潛力。

研究團隊進行了一系列的實驗，包括流行病學觀察、分子機制的探討以及臨床試驗。他們分析了食用青花椰菜和其他十字花科蔬菜與癌症風險之間的關聯，並進一步研究蘿蔔硫素如何影響細胞內的生化反應。臨床試驗的部分則評估了蘿蔔硫素對癌症生物標記物的影響，並探討其在代謝解毒過程中的潛在作用，這些研究為未來的應用提供了重要的科學依據。

主要的研究發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能有助於降低某些致癌生物標記物的水平，並支持身體的代謝解毒過程。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素在癌症預防和治療中可能具有一定的潛力，但仍需進一步的研究來確認其效果。此外，研究也指出了在將蘿蔔硫素的療法從實驗室轉向臨床應用時所面臨的挑戰，例如生物利用度的限制、劑量的優化以及青花椰菜衍生產品的標準化等問題。

title_en: Sulforaphane Pre-Treatment Improves Alveolar Macrophage Killing After Alcohol-Induced Phagocytic Dysfunction In Vitro and in Galleria mellonella Larvae.
abstract_en: Background: Alcohol is associated with increased mortality and morbidity globally. Pulmonary infections with opportunistic pathogens can occur in healthy humans; however, binge alcohol intoxication (≥0.08% BAC) is a major risk factor. We have previously shown that a single dose of alcohol comparable to binge alcohol intoxication increases infection by reducing alveolar macrophage function in vivo. Sulforaphane (SFN), a phytonutrient, is a potent inducer of antioxidant production through the induction of nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) and inhibition of the nuclear factor kappa-light-chain-enhancer (NF-kB) pathway. The aim of this study was to test the therapeutic potential of SFN given as a pretreatment to prevent alcohol-induced phagocytic dysfunction. Methods: Intracellular phagocytic killing was measured via colony-forming units (CFU) and cytokine expression via ELISA. G. mellonella survival was used to determine the therapeutic potential of SFN in vivo. Results: Dose-response curves indicated that SFN concentrations of less than 20 µM were not cytotoxic in either MH-S (murine) or THP-1 (human) cells. Live infection assay results showed that MH-S and THP-1 cells pretreated with SFN (5 µM) and challenged with 0.2% (v/v) alcohol for 3 or 8 h prior to live B. thailandensis or S. epidermidis infection improved intracellular pathogen killing between 12- and 20-fold compared to macrophages treated with alcohol alone. ELISA analysis indicated that SFN significantly reduced levels of Tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α) expression at 3 and 8 h compared to controls. Additionally, a Galleria mellonella larvae model demonstrated greater survivability in the prophylaxis group compared to larvae exposed to either Gram-positive or Gram-negative pathogens, as well as in groups that received alcohol prior to pathogen inoculation. Conclusions: Taken together, SFN-induced cytoprotection was extended beyond in vitro cell culture to include an in vivo G. mellonella model demonstrating protection against Gram-positive and negative opportunistic pathogens. These data demonstrate that SFN may be an effective pretreatment option to prevent alcohol-mediated innate immune dysfunction and restore macrophage phagocytic killing.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些有關健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是針對酒精對免疫系統的影響。酒精的過量攝取會增加感染的風險，這對於健康人來說也是一個潛在的威脅。研究指出，蘿蔔硫素作為一種植物營養素，可能有助於減少酒精引起的免疫功能障礙，這意味著透過適當的營養補充，或許能在某種程度上保護我們的身體免受酒精的負面影響，進而降低感染的風險。這對於那些偶爾飲酒或有飲酒習慣的人來說，可能是一個值得關注的健康訊息。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在預防酒精引起的吞噬功能障礙中的潛在療效。研究者關心的問題是，當人類或動物在攝取酒精後，免疫系統的吞噬細胞功能會受到影響，而蘿蔔硫素是否能作為一種預防措施來改善這種情況。這項研究希望能找出一種有效的方法，來減少酒精對免疫系統的負面影響，並提高身體對感染的抵抗力。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者使用了不同的細胞模型，包括小鼠的MH-S細胞和人類的THP-1細胞，來測試蘿蔔硫素的效果。研究者首先測量了細胞的吞噬能力和細胞因子的表達，並利用G. mellonella（大蠟蟲）作為活體模型來評估蘿蔔硫素的療效。研究中，細胞在接受蘿蔔硫素處理後，再暴露於酒精和病原體，觀察其對病原體的清除能力，這樣的設計能夠幫助研究者了解蘿蔔硫素的保護作用。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素在低濃度下不會對細胞造成毒性，並且能顯著提高細胞對病原體的清除能力。特別是，經過蘿蔔硫素處理的細胞在面對酒精挑戰後，對病原體的吞噬能力提升了12到20倍。此外，G. mellonella模型的實驗結果也顯示，接受蘿蔔硫素預防處理的幼蟲在面對病原體時存活率更高。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能是一種有效的預防措施，能改善酒精引起的免疫功能障礙，並恢復吞噬細胞的功能。

本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在預防酒精引起的吞噬功能障礙中的潛在療效。研究者關心的問題是，當人類或動物在攝取酒精後，免疫系統的吞噬細胞功能會受到影響，而蘿蔔硫素是否能作為一種預防措施來改善這種情況。這項研究希望能找出一種有效的方法，來減少酒精對免疫系統的負面影響，並提高身體對感染的抵抗力。

在實驗中，研究者使用了不同的細胞模型，包括小鼠的MH-S細胞和人類的THP-1細胞，來測試蘿蔔硫素的效果。研究者首先測量了細胞的吞噬能力和細胞因子的表達，並利用G. mellonella（大蠟蟲）作為活體模型來評估蘿蔔硫素的療效。研究中，細胞在接受蘿蔔硫素處理後，再暴露於酒精和病原體，觀察其對病原體的清除能力，這樣的設計能夠幫助研究者了解蘿蔔硫素的保護作用。

title_en: Targeted activation of Nrf2 via sulforaphane-loaded exosomes attenuated azoospermic condition in the rat model.
abstract_en: Azoospermia, the complete absence of sperm in the ejaculate, presents a major barrier to male fertility. Oxidative stress and impaired cellular homeostasis are key contributors to germ cell loss, particularly in chemotherapy-induced azoospermia. Sulforaphane (SFN), a potent activator of the Nrf2 pathway, offers antioxidant benefits, but its systemic delivery is limited by bioavailability and potential reductive stress. This study aimed to evaluate the regenerative potential of SFN-loaded exosomes (SFN + EXO) in a rat model of azoospermia. Human serum-derived exosomes were isolated, characterized and engineered to encapsulate SFN. Azoospermia was induced in Wistar rats via intratesticular busulfan injection. Animals were assigned to five groups: healthy control, azoospermic control, SFN, exosomes (EXO) and SFN + EXO. Spermatogenesis parameters, histopathology, testosterone levels, oxidative stress markers and gene expression of Nrf2, autophagy and germ cell markers were evaluated. SFN + EXO treatment significantly improved sperm count, motility, morphology and testis weight index compared to controls and monotherapy groups. Histological recovery of spermatogenic lineages was superior in SFN + EXO rats, accompanied by reduced fibrosis and normalized testicular architecture. Expression of DAZL and VASA was fully restored, while aberrant upregulation of Nrf2 and autophagy genes (LC3, Beclin1, p62) in azoospermic testes was normalized only by SFN + EXO. Antioxidant enzyme activity (GPx, TAC) was significantly enhanced, suggesting redox balance recovery. Local delivery of sulforaphane via exosomes effectively reverses chemotherapy-induced spermatogenic failure through modulation of oxidative stress and autophagy, promoting germ cell regeneration. This exosome-based platform offers a promising therapeutic avenue for male infertility. The online version contains supplementary material available at 10.1038/s41598-026-40709-x.
para1: 這項研究對於男性不孕症的預防和治療提供了一些新的啟示。男性不孕症的主要原因之一是精子缺乏，而這通常與氧化壓力和細胞內環境失衡有關。特別是在接受化療的男性中，這種情況更為常見。研究顯示，透過使用蘿蔔硫素來改善這些問題，可能有助於促進精子的生成，從而提高男性的生育能力。這對於希望成為父親的男性來說，無疑是一個值得關注的方向。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素載體外泌體在大鼠模型中對無精症的再生潛力。無精症是指精液中完全沒有精子的情況，這對男性的生育能力造成了重大障礙。研究者希望了解蘿蔔硫素如何透過外泌體的方式來改善無精症的情況，並評估其對精子生成的影響。
para3: 在實驗中，研究人員使用了Wistar大鼠作為模型，首先通過注射busulfan來誘導無精症。然後，將大鼠分為五組，包括健康對照組、無精症對照組、僅蘿蔔硫素組、僅外泌體組和蘿蔔硫素加外泌體組。研究者對各組的大鼠進行了精子生成參數、組織病理學、睾酮水平、氧化壓力標記和基因表達等多方面的評估，以了解不同處理對無精症的影響。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素加外泌體組在精子數量、活動力、形態及睾丸重量指數等方面顯著優於其他對照組和單一治療組。組織學檢查顯示，這組大鼠的精子生成線索恢復情況更佳，伴隨著纖維化減少和睾丸結構的正常化。此外，某些基因的表達也得到了恢復，顯示出蘿蔔硫素加外泌體在改善氧化壓力和自噬方面的潛力，這可能有助於促進生殖細胞的再生。這一基於外泌體的平台為男性不孕症的治療提供了一個有前景的方向。
explanation_zh:
這項研究對於男性不孕症的預防和治療提供了一些新的啟示。男性不孕症的主要原因之一是精子缺乏，而這通常與氧化壓力和細胞內環境失衡有關。特別是在接受化療的男性中，這種情況更為常見。研究顯示，透過使用蘿蔔硫素來改善這些問題，可能有助於促進精子的生成，從而提高男性的生育能力。這對於希望成為父親的男性來說，無疑是一個值得關注的方向。

這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素載體外泌體在大鼠模型中對無精症的再生潛力。無精症是指精液中完全沒有精子的情況，這對男性的生育能力造成了重大障礙。研究者希望了解蘿蔔硫素如何透過外泌體的方式來改善無精症的情況，並評估其對精子生成的影響。

在實驗中，研究人員使用了Wistar大鼠作為模型，首先通過注射busulfan來誘導無精症。然後，將大鼠分為五組，包括健康對照組、無精症對照組、僅蘿蔔硫素組、僅外泌體組和蘿蔔硫素加外泌體組。研究者對各組的大鼠進行了精子生成參數、組織病理學、睾酮水平、氧化壓力標記和基因表達等多方面的評估，以了解不同處理對無精症的影響。

研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素加外泌體組在精子數量、活動力、形態及睾丸重量指數等方面顯著優於其他對照組和單一治療組。組織學檢查顯示，這組大鼠的精子生成線索恢復情況更佳，伴隨著纖維化減少和睾丸結構的正常化。此外，某些基因的表達也得到了恢復，顯示出蘿蔔硫素加外泌體在改善氧化壓力和自噬方面的潛力，這可能有助於促進生殖細胞的再生。這一基於外泌體的平台為男性不孕症的治療提供了一個有前景的方向。

研究團隊使用大鼠進行實驗，首先讓它們出現無精症的情況，然後分成幾組進行不同的處理，包括單獨使用蘿蔔硫素和外泌體（細胞間的信號傳遞物質）。他們觀察了精子生成的參數和其他生理指標。

結果發現，蘿蔔硫素加外泌體的組別在精子數量、活動力和睾丸健康等方面，都顯著優於其他組別。這表示這種組合可能在改善男性生育能力方面有潛力！

title_en: Protective Effects of Curcumin and Sulforaphane Against Ionising Radiation-Induced Oxidative Stress and Inflammatory Responses in Rat Lung Tissue.
abstract_en: Ionising radiation-induced lung injury is a major complication of thoracic radiotherapy, primarily driven by oxidative stress and inflammation. The current study evaluates and compares the protective effects of sulforaphane (SFN) and curcumin (CUR) pretreatment against radiation-induced oxidative damage and inflammation in rat lung tissue. Female Wistar rats were pretreated in vivo with SFN (2 mg/kg b.w./day) or CUR (4.13 mg/kg b.w./day) for 28 days per os. Isolated lung tissues were exposed ex vivo to γ-radiation (absorbed dose: 2 Gy). Oxidative stress markers-malondialdehyde (MDA), ischemia-modified albumin (IMA), total sulfhydryl (SH) groups, reduced glutathione (GSH), and superoxide dismutase (SOD)-and inflammatory markers-tumour necrosis factor alpha (TNF-α), prostaglandin-endoperoxide synthase 2 (PTGS2/COX-2), interleukin-6 (IL-6), and interleukin-1 beta (IL-1β)-were measured to evaluate irradiation and protective effects. Radiation significantly increased MDA, TNF-α, PTGS2/COX-2, and IL-6 levels while decreasing SH groups. Pretreatment with SFN or CUR attenuated these changes. CUR showed a more pronounced effect on oxidative stress-related parameters, whereas SFN more strongly influenced inflammatory markers. These findings suggest that SFN and CUR differentially modulate radiation-induced oxidative and inflammatory responses in lung tissue under the applied experimental conditions and warrant further investigation of their potential as protective agents in radiotherapy.
para1: 這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，輻射引起的肺部損傷是一個重要的問題，尤其是在接受胸部放射治療的病人中。這種損傷主要是由氧化壓力和發炎反應所驅動。因此，尋找能夠減輕這些損傷的保護劑，對於改善放射治療的安全性和效果非常重要。研究中探討的蘿蔔硫素和薑黃素，可能成為未來輻射治療中有用的輔助療法，幫助減少副作用，提升病人的生活品質。
para2: 這項研究的主題是評估和比較蘿蔔硫素和薑黃素在輻射引起的氧化損傷和發炎中的保護效果。研究者關心的問題是，這兩種天然化合物是否能有效減少輻射對肺部組織造成的損害，並且它們的作用機制是否有所不同。透過這項研究，科學家希望能更深入了解這些化合物在輻射治療中的潛在應用。
para3: 實驗中使用了雌性Wistar大鼠作為研究對象，這些大鼠在實驗前接受了蘿蔔硫素或薑黃素的預處理，持續28天。蘿蔔硫素的劑量為每天每公斤體重2毫克，而薑黃素則為每天每公斤體重4.13毫克。隨後，研究者將分離的肺組織暴露於伽馬輻射下，劑量為2 Gy，並測量氧化壓力和發炎的相關指標，以評估輻射的影響及這兩種化合物的保護效果。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，輻射顯著增加了氧化壓力標記物和發炎標記物的水平，而預處理蘿蔔硫素或薑黃素則能減輕這些變化。特別是，薑黃素對氧化壓力相關的參數影響較為明顯，而蘿蔔硫素則對發炎標記物的影響更強。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素和薑黃素在輻射引起的氧化和發炎反應中具有不同的調節作用，值得進一步研究它們作為輻射治療保護劑的潛力。

title_en: Exogenous myrosinase from mustard seed increases bioavailability of sulforaphane from a glucoraphanin-rich broccoli seed extract in a randomized clinical study.
abstract_en: Inactive glucoraphanin (GR) in broccoli is converted to the antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-bacterial sulforaphane (SF) by cruciferous vegetable enzyme myrosinase (Myr), or similar enzymes from specific gut bacteria; both sources have variable efficiency. The effects of exogenous Myr on the conversion efficiency of GR to SF was compared to gut microbial Myr-like activity. In a randomized, double-blind, crossover study, sixteen subjects (9 F: 7 M) received a single oral dose of GR in broccoli seed extract with Myr-containing mustard seed powder, or broccoli seed extract alone, both with ascorbic acid. GR + Myr, on average, doubled the bioavailability of SF (39.8 ± 3.1%) compared to GR alone (18.6 ± 3.1%), and increased the conversion rate in the first 8 h (25.4% ± 2.7%) compared to GR alone (8.0% ± 2.7) based on measurement of urinary metabolites. There were no differences in fecal bacterial communities after the single dose; however, four bacterial GR-converting genes significantly correlated with GR conversion (p < 0.0155). To our knowledge, this is the first human study to simultaneously investigate (1) a well-defined Myr source, (2) broccoli seeds as source of GR, (3) prediction of gut microbial responsiveness to GR.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些關於健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是針對抗氧化和抗發炎的效果。青花椰菜中的蘿蔔硫素在轉化過程中，可能對我們的身體健康有正面的影響。透過這項研究，我們可以更了解如何提高蘿蔔硫素的生物可利用性，進而可能增強其在預防某些疾病方面的潛力。這對於希望透過飲食改善健康的人來說，無疑是一個值得關注的方向。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討青花椰菜中的蘿蔔硫素如何轉化為具有抗氧化、抗發炎和抗菌特性的活性成分。研究者特別關注不同來源的酶（例如青花椰菜中的酶和腸道細菌中的類似酶）對於蘿蔔硫素轉化效率的影響。這個問題的解決不僅能幫助我們理解青花椰菜的健康益處，還能為未來的營養研究提供新的視角。
para3: 在這項研究中，研究者進行了一個隨機、雙盲、交叉的實驗，招募了16名受試者（9名女性和7名男性）。受試者接受了單次口服的青花椰菜種子提取物，並分別搭配含有酶的芥菜種子粉或僅青花椰菜種子提取物，兩者皆添加了抗壞血酸。研究者測量了受試者尿液中的代謝物，以評估蘿蔔硫素的生物可利用性和轉化率。
para4: 研究結果顯示，搭配酶的青花椰菜種子提取物能顯著提高蘿蔔硫素的生物可利用性，平均達到39.8%，而僅使用青花椰菜種子提取物的生物可利用性則為18.6%。在前8小時內，轉化率也有明顯提升，分別為25.4%和8.0%。雖然單次劑量後糞便中的細菌群落沒有顯著變化，但有四個與蘿蔔硫素轉化相關的細菌基因顯示出顯著的相關性，這為未來的研究提供了新的線索。

title_en: Sulforaphane Against the Metabolic Consequences of a High-Glycemic-Index Diet: Protective and Therapeutic Mechanisms Associated with Obesity and Insulin Resistance.
abstract_en: Objective: This study investigated the effects of different doses and timing of sulforaphane (SFN) supplementation on reducing obesity induced by a high-glycemic-index diet (HGID) and on correcting poor glycemic control and dyslipidemia in C57BL/6 mice. Method: For 15 weeks, mice were administered a control diet (control), HGID, HGID + oral 5 mg/kg/day SFN (HGID + LSFN), or HGID + 20 mg/kg/day SFN (HGID + HSFN), and following 15 weeks of HGID, mice were treated with 5 mg/kg/day SFN (PO-HGID + LSFN) or 20 mg/kg/day SFN (PO-HGID + HSFN) for 5 weeks. Results: SFN reduced body weight gain and serum glucose. The lowest levels of HbA1c were observed in the control and HGID + LSFN groups. Mice in the HGID group exhibited impaired glucose clearance and were less sensitive to insulin compared to the control. A remarkable improvement in glucose and insulin tolerance was observed in both PO-HGID + SFN and HGID + SFN groups. Lipid profile parameters and serum insulin levels were found to be lower in the control and HGID + SFN groups compared to the HGID group. SFN increased serum adiponectin levels when administered concurrently with HGID. IRS1 and IRS2 levels were highest in the control and HGID + LSFN groups, and high-dose SFN supplementation suppressed IRS1 independently of timing. Exposure to HGID downregulated the expression of PGC-1α and sirtuins. SIRT1 and SIRT3 gene expressions showed a significant increase at both doses, whereas SIRT2 gene expression increased significantly only at 5 mg/kg/day SFN. FASN expression was upregulated in all HGID-fed groups with or without SFN intervention. Conclusions: SFN may reverse the adverse effects of HGID in a time- and dose-dependent manner by regulating postprandial insulin, inhibiting gluconeogenesis, and enhancing fatty acid oxidation through the activation of sirtuins and PGC-1α.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些關於如何透過飲食補充來改善健康的啟示，特別是針對因高升糖指數飲食而引起的肥胖問題。研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素的補充可能有助於改善血糖控制和脂質異常，這對於預防與肥胖相關的健康問題，如糖尿病和心血管疾病，具有潛在的意義。透過適當的劑量和時間安排，蘿蔔硫素可能成為一種有效的輔助療法，幫助人們維持健康的體重和良好的代謝狀態。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討不同劑量和時間的蘿蔔硫素補充對於由高升糖指數飲食引起的肥胖、血糖控制不良和脂質異常的影響。研究特別關注這些因素如何影響小鼠的健康狀態，並試圖找出最佳的補充方式，以改善這些由飲食引起的健康問題。
para3: 實驗中使用了C57BL/6小鼠，為期15週的研究中，這些小鼠被分為幾組，接受不同的飲食：控制飲食、高升糖指數飲食、以及不同劑量的蘿蔔硫素補充。研究者在小鼠接受高升糖指數飲食後，進一步給予蘿蔔硫素進行為期5週的治療，以觀察其對體重、血糖和脂質的影響。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素能夠減少小鼠的體重增加和血清葡萄糖水平。與控制組相比，接受高升糖指數飲食的小鼠在葡萄糖清除能力和胰島素敏感性上表現不佳，但在接受蘿蔔硫素後，這些指標有顯著改善。此外，研究還發現蘿蔔硫素能夠調節胰島素的分泌，並促進脂肪酸的氧化，這些結果顯示蘿蔔硫素可能在改善代謝健康方面具有潛力。

這項研究提供了一些關於如何透過飲食補充來改善健康的啟示，特別是針對因高升糖指數飲食而引起的肥胖問題。研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素的補充可能有助於改善血糖控制和脂質異常，這對於預防與肥胖相關的健康問題，如糖尿病和心血管疾病，具有潛在的意義。透過適當的劑量和時間安排，蘿蔔硫素可能成為一種有效的輔助療法，幫助人們維持健康的體重和良好的代謝狀態。

這項研究的主題是探討不同劑量和時間的蘿蔔硫素補充對於由高升糖指數飲食引起的肥胖、血糖控制不良和脂質異常的影響。研究特別關注這些因素如何影響小鼠的健康狀態，並試圖找出最佳的補充方式，以改善這些由飲食引起的健康問題。

實驗中使用了C57BL/6小鼠，為期15週的研究中，這些小鼠被分為幾組，接受不同的飲食：控制飲食、高升糖指數飲食、以及不同劑量的蘿蔔硫素補充。研究者在小鼠接受高升糖指數飲食後，進一步給予蘿蔔硫素進行為期5週的治療，以觀察其對體重、血糖和脂質的影響。

研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素能夠減少小鼠的體重增加和血清葡萄糖水平。與控制組相比，接受高升糖指數飲食的小鼠在葡萄糖清除能力和胰島素敏感性上表現不佳，但在接受蘿蔔硫素後，這些指標有顯著改善。此外，研究還發現蘿蔔硫素能夠調節胰島素的分泌，並促進脂肪酸的氧化，這些結果顯示蘿蔔硫素可能在改善代謝健康方面具有潛力。

title_en: Sulforaphane-Mediated Multitarget Therapeutic Effects in Methylmercury-Induced ALS-Like Pathology: Comparative Analysis and Multifaceted Approach to Neuroprotection and Systemic Recovery.
abstract_en: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder marked by motor neuron loss driven by oxidative stress, neuroinflammation, and dysregulated survival signaling. The objective of this study was to evaluate the neuroprotective efficacy and safety of sulforaphane (SUFP) in a methylmercury (MMHg⁺)-induced preclinical rat model of ALS, with comparison to omaveloxolone (OVX) and dimethyl fumarate (DIMT). SUFP treatment, particularly at 4 mg/kg, significantly restored antioxidant defense mechanisms through upregulation of Nrf2, HO-1, and SIRT1 while suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-1β, TNF-α), apoptotic markers (Bax, caspase-3), and stress-related signaling pathways including p75NTR, PI3K/Akt, and MAPKs. These molecular effects translated into meaningful functional recovery, as evidenced by improvements in grip strength, locomotor performance, spatial memory, and depressive-like behavior. Histopathological evaluation demonstrated attenuation of demyelination and preservation of neuronal architecture in cortical, hippocampal, and cerebellar regions. Beyond central neuroprotection, SUFP exerted systemic benefits by normalizing hepatic enzymes, improving skeletal muscle integrity, restoring redox balance, stabilizing neurofilament and myelin-associated proteins, and correcting hematological alterations. Comparative analysis revealed that SUFP conferred superior neuroprotection with a favorable safety profile relative to OVX and, although slightly less efficacious than DIMT, exhibited reduced systemic toxicity. Molecular docking further supported SUFP's interaction with Nrf2-Keap1 targets, reinforcing its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory mechanisms. Collectively, these findings identify SUFP as a multifaceted and well-tolerated therapeutic candidate for ALS, supporting its further translational and clinical evaluation.

title_en: Mechanistic insights into PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced myopia in mice: Role of NRF2/HIF-1α signaling and therapeutic potential of sulforaphane.
abstract_en: Particulate matter 2.5 (PM<sub>2.5</sub>) has been implicated in the development of myopia. This study aimed to elucidate the molecular mechanisms through which PM<sub>2.5</sub> exposure influences myopia progression. We integrated Mendelian randomization (MR) analysis of large-scale genetic data, revealing a significant association between particulate matter air pollution (PMAP) and myopia risk (OR = 1.065, 95 % CI: 1.033-1.098; P = 4.73× 10⁻⁵). In mice, ocular PM<sub>2.5</sub> exposure induced pathological axial elongation, accompanied by suppression of NRF2 expression. Mechanistic investigations demonstrated that NRF2 directly binds to HIF-1α, inhibiting its transcriptional activity. Using CETSA, we identified sulforaphane as a selective NRF2 stabilizer that mitigates PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced myopia progression in mice. These findings present genetic epidemiological evidence supporting a causal association between PMAP exposure and myopia, elucidate a molecular mechanism by which PM<sub>2.5</sub> mediates oxidative stress via the NRF2/HIF-1α signaling axis, and identify sulforaphane as a potential therapeutic agent targeting NRF2, thereby offering novel strategies for the prevention and treatment of environmentally associated myopia.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些重要的啟示，特別是與近視的發展有關。研究顯示，空氣中的細懸浮微粒（PM2.5）可能會影響我們的視力，進而導致近視的加重。這提醒我們，保持良好的空氣品質對於眼睛健康至關重要，尤其是在城市地區，空氣污染的問題更為嚴重。因此，了解這些環境因素如何影響我們的健康，能幫助我們採取更有效的預防措施，保護視力，減少近視的風險。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討細懸浮微粒（PM2.5）與近視之間的關聯。研究者希望了解PM2.5如何影響近視的進展，並揭示其背後的分子機制。近視是一種常見的視力問題，隨著生活方式的改變，特別是長時間使用電子產品，近視的發病率逐年上升。因此，了解環境因素對近視的影響，對於制定有效的預防策略具有重要意義。
para3: 在這項研究中，研究者使用了大規模的基因數據進行孟德爾隨機化分析，以探討PM2.5與近視風險之間的關聯。此外，研究還在小鼠身上進行了實驗，觀察PM2.5的眼部暴露如何導致病理性眼軸延長，並檢測NRF2表達的變化。這些實驗幫助研究者理解PM2.5如何透過特定的分子途徑影響近視的發展，並尋找可能的治療方法。
para4: 研究的主要發現顯示，PM2.5的暴露與近視風險之間存在顯著的關聯，並且在小鼠實驗中，PM2.5的眼部暴露會導致眼軸延長，這與NRF2的表達抑制有關。此外，研究者發現蘿蔔硫素可以作為NRF2的選擇性穩定劑，減緩PM2.5引起的近視進展。這些結果提供了基因流行病學的證據，支持PM2.5暴露與近視之間的因果關係，並揭示了NRF2/HIF-1α信號通路在氧化壓力中的作用，為環境相關近視的預防和治療提供了新的策略。
explanation_zh:
這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些重要的啟示，特別是與近視的發展有關。研究顯示，空氣中的細懸浮微粒（PM2.5）可能會影響我們的視力，進而導致近視的加重。這提醒我們，保持良好的空氣品質對於眼睛健康至關重要，尤其是在城市地區，空氣污染的問題更為嚴重。因此，了解這些環境因素如何影響我們的健康，能幫助我們採取更有效的預防措施，保護視力，減少近視的風險。

這項研究的主題是探討細懸浮微粒（PM2.5）與近視之間的關聯。研究者希望了解PM2.5如何影響近視的進展，並揭示其背後的分子機制。近視是一種常見的視力問題，隨著生活方式的改變，特別是長時間使用電子產品，近視的發病率逐年上升。因此，了解環境因素對近視的影響，對於制定有效的預防策略具有重要意義。

在這項研究中，研究者使用了大規模的基因數據進行孟德爾隨機化分析，以探討PM2.5與近視風險之間的關聯。此外，研究還在小鼠身上進行了實驗，觀察PM2.5的眼部暴露如何導致病理性眼軸延長，並檢測NRF2表達的變化。這些實驗幫助研究者理解PM2.5如何透過特定的分子途徑影響近視的發展，並尋找可能的治療方法。

研究的主要發現顯示，PM2.5的暴露與近視風險之間存在顯著的關聯，並且在小鼠實驗中，PM2.5的眼部暴露會導致眼軸延長，這與NRF2的表達抑制有關。此外，研究者發現蘿蔔硫素可以作為NRF2的選擇性穩定劑，減緩PM2.5引起的近視進展。這些結果提供了基因流行病學的證據，支持PM2.5暴露與近視之間的因果關係，並揭示了NRF2/HIF-1α信號通路在氧化壓力中的作用，為環境相關近視的預防和治療提供了新的策略。

這項研究專注於PM2.5與近視之間的關聯。研究者想知道這些微粒如何影響近視的發展，並揭示了背後的分子機制。隨著科技進步，越來越多人長時間使用電子產品，近視的問題變得更加普遍。因此，了解環境因素對近視的影響是制定有效預防策略的關鍵。

title_en: Efficacy and mechanisms of cisplatin and sulforaphane nanoparticles in alleviating cisplatin resistance in non-small cell lung cancer.
abstract_en: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) is the most common type of lung cancer, and its morbidity and mortality rates continue to increase, placing tremendous pressure on global public health systems. Cisplatin (DDP), also known as diamminedichloroplatinum (II), is a traditional chemotherapy drug widely used in the treatment of NSCLC; however, it has serious side effects and can easily lead to drug resistance, which seriously limits its efficacy. The mechanism of DDP resistance is closely related to ferroptosis. Tumor cells evade the killing effect of drugs by regulating iron metabolism, anti-oxidant responses, and lipid metabolism. Sulforaphane (SFN) is a classic ferroptosis activator with potent anti-tumor effects. This study aims to develop a novel NSCLC-targeted nanoplatform loaded with cisplatin and sulforaphane, which is expected to reverse the drug resistance induced by cisplatin monotherapy. This study was conducted on three levels: materials science, cytology and zoology. First, nanostructured SFN and DDP particles (nSDDPs) with NSCLC-targeting functions were synthesized. The distribution characteristics and safety characteristics were evaluated through cell experiments and animal experiments using immunofluorescence imaging, in vivo imaging technology, and hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining. To explore the efficacy of the nSDDPs, we tested the cell activity, cell proliferation rate, cell scratch, and ferroptosis-related indicators. To further test the DDP resistance-improving effect and anti-tumor effect of the nSDDPs, we conducted subcutaneous tumor-bearing experiments to detect tumor size, assess histological status, investigate metastasis-related factors and ferroptosis-related indicators. The synthesized nSDDPs showed good targeting and biocompatibility in cell and animal models of NSCLC. In the cell experiments, the nSDDPs exerted a significant inhibitory effect on cell activity, reduced the cell proliferation rate, and exerted a strong growth inhibition effect in the cell scratch assays. In addition, the nSDDPs also exerted a significant activation effect on the ferroptosis-related indicators, further enhancing the anti-tumor effect. In the animal experiments, the nSDDPs significantly inhibited tumor growth. The tumor volume and weight were smaller, and the necrosis and apoptosis of the tumor tissue were more obvious in the nSDDP group than in the DDP group. The expression of metastasis-related factors was also significantly decreased, indicating that the nSDDPs had a strong effect in improving DDP resistance and inhibiting tumor metastasis. The nSDDPs exerted good anti-tumor effects and overcame DDP resistance to a certain extent, providing a new strategy for the treatment of NSCLC. The nSDDPs which were successfully synthesized in this study improved DDP resistance by activating the ferroptosis pathway and also exerted anti-tumor effects. Our findings provide new insights into the study of DDP drug resistance.
para1: 非小細胞肺癌是最常見的肺癌類型，其發病率和死亡率持續上升，對全球公共衛生系統造成了巨大的壓力。傳統的化療藥物順鉑雖然廣泛用於治療非小細胞肺癌，但其副作用嚴重，且容易導致藥物抗性，這限制了其療效。因此，尋找能夠改善藥物抗性的治療方法，對於提升患者的健康狀況和預防疾病的進一步發展具有重要意義。
para2: 本研究的主題是針對非小細胞肺癌的藥物抗性問題，探索一種新型的納米平台，該平台結合了順鉑和蘿蔔硫素，旨在逆轉由順鉑單一療法引起的藥物抗性。研究關注的重點是如何通過這種新型納米平台來增強治療效果，並克服目前治療中的挑戰。
para3: 實驗分為三個層面進行：材料科學、細胞學和動物學。首先，合成了具有非小細胞肺癌靶向功能的納米結構蘿蔔硫素和順鉑顆粒。接著，通過細胞實驗和動物實驗，利用免疫熒光成像、體內成像技術和組織染色等方法評估其分佈特徵和安全性。為了探討納米平台的療效，我們測試了細胞活性、細胞增殖率、細胞刮傷和與鐵死亡相關的指標。
para4: 主要發現顯示，合成的納米平台在細胞和動物模型中展現出良好的靶向性和生物相容性。在細胞實驗中，納米平台顯著抑制了細胞活性，降低了細胞增殖率，並在細胞刮傷實驗中顯示出強大的生長抑制效果。此外，納米平台還顯著激活了與鐵死亡相關的指標，進一步增強了抗腫瘤效果。在動物實驗中，納米平台顯著抑制了腫瘤生長，腫瘤體積和重量較小，腫瘤組織的壞死和凋亡現象也更為明顯，顯示出改善順鉑抗性和抑制腫瘤轉移的潛力。
explanation_zh:
非小細胞肺癌是最常見的肺癌類型，其發病率和死亡率持續上升，對全球公共衛生系統造成了巨大的壓力。傳統的化療藥物順鉑雖然廣泛用於治療非小細胞肺癌，但其副作用嚴重，且容易導致藥物抗性，這限制了其療效。因此，尋找能夠改善藥物抗性的治療方法，對於提升患者的健康狀況和預防疾病的進一步發展具有重要意義。

本研究的主題是針對非小細胞肺癌的藥物抗性問題，探索一種新型的納米平台，該平台結合了順鉑和蘿蔔硫素，旨在逆轉由順鉑單一療法引起的藥物抗性。研究關注的重點是如何通過這種新型納米平台來增強治療效果，並克服目前治療中的挑戰。

實驗分為三個層面進行：材料科學、細胞學和動物學。首先，合成了具有非小細胞肺癌靶向功能的納米結構蘿蔔硫素和順鉑顆粒。接著，通過細胞實驗和動物實驗，利用免疫熒光成像、體內成像技術和組織染色等方法評估其分佈特徵和安全性。為了探討納米平台的療效，我們測試了細胞活性、細胞增殖率、細胞刮傷和與鐵死亡相關的指標。

主要發現顯示，合成的納米平台在細胞和動物模型中展現出良好的靶向性和生物相容性。在細胞實驗中，納米平台顯著抑制了細胞活性，降低了細胞增殖率，並在細胞刮傷實驗中顯示出強大的生長抑制效果。此外，納米平台還顯著激活了與鐵死亡相關的指標，進一步增強了抗腫瘤效果。在動物實驗中，納米平台顯著抑制了腫瘤生長，腫瘤體積和重量較小，腫瘤組織的壞死和凋亡現象也更為明顯，顯示出改善順鉑抗性和抑制腫瘤轉移的潛力。

根據《Translational lung cancer research》於 2026 年 1 月 發表的論文，🌱 你知道嗎？我們的飲食中有些食物可能對抗癌有幫助！最近的研究聚焦於一種叫「蘿蔔硫素」的成分，這是一種在蘿蔔和青花椰菜中找到的天然物質。這項研究主要想解決非小細胞肺癌患者在使用化療藥物時常遇到的抗藥性問題。  
  
研究團隊創造了一個新型的納米平台，將順鉑（常用的化療藥物）和蘿蔔硫素結合在一起，目的是希望能夠改善化療的效果，並克服抗藥性。  
  
實驗分為三個部分進行：首先，他們合成了具有針對非小細胞肺癌的納米結構，接著進行細胞和動物實驗，評估這些納米平台的安全性和效果。  
  
最終，研究發現這個納米平台在細胞和動物模型中表現出良好的效果，能夠顯著抑制癌細胞的增長，並激活與細胞死亡相關的指標，顯示出它在抗癌方面的潛力。  
  
🔍 主要重點：  
1. 蘿蔔硫素可能有助於改善肺癌患者的化療效果。  
2. 新型納米平台在實驗中顯示出良好的安全性和效果。  
3. 這項研究為未來的癌症治療提供了新的思路！
